title: Stateright foundation: production harness + drift guard
description:
**Problem**
Stateright models are isolated in `beads_stateright_models` with toy types and custom logic. There is no compile-time guarantee that models stay in sync with production, and no shared harness to reuse production code paths.

**Context**
- Prod logic to reuse: `src/core/event.rs`, `src/core/watermark.rs`, `src/core/apply.rs`, `src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs`, `src/daemon/repl/peer_acks.rs`, `src/daemon/durability_coordinator.rs`, `src/daemon/wal/*`, `src/test_harness/mod.rs`
- Stateright actor system + network semantics: `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/src/actor.rs`, `src/actor/model.rs`, `src/actor/network.rs`

**Goal**
Create a minimal production-backed modeling harness and a drift guard so any prod change breaks model compilation.
design:
**Design**
1) Add a feature-gated modeling harness in the main crate (e.g. `feature = "model-testing"`).
   - New module `src/model/` that exposes only *pure* adapters used by Stateright models.
   - APIs should wrap production functions, not re-implement logic.
   - Example helpers:
     - `model::event_factory` → build canonical `EventBody` + `EventFrameV1` using `encode_event_body_canonical`.
     - `model::repl_ingest` → thin wrappers around `verify_event_frame`, `GapBufferByNsOrigin`, `Watermark` helpers.
     - `model::durability` → wrapper around `PeerAckTable` + `DurabilityCoordinator::poll_replicated`.
     - `model::state_digest` → hash/summary of `CanonicalState` (if needed for model state hashability).
2) Update `beads_stateright_models/Cargo.toml` to depend on the main crate via a path dep with `default-features = false` + `features = ["model-testing"]`.
3) Add a compile-time drift guard in `beads_stateright_models` (a small module that imports all production types/functions referenced by models). If any signature changes, models should fail to compile.
4) Add a CI/slow-tests command to compile the models crate (e.g. `cargo check -p beads_stateright_models`).
5) Document the no-drift rule in `beads_stateright_models/README.md`.

**Notes**
Keep the harness tiny and feature-gated so normal builds don’t pull daemon deps.
acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] `beads-rs` exposes a `model-testing` (or similar) feature with a `src/model/*` harness of production-backed adapters.
- [ ] `beads_stateright_models` depends on the main crate via path dep and compiles with `feature = model-testing`.
- [ ] A drift-guard module in the models crate imports all production APIs used by models and fails compilation on signature changes.
- [ ] Documented command to compile models in CI/slow-tests.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Encode core namespace presence in StoreState type
description:
**Problem**
`StoreState` can exist without the core namespace present. The map is just `BTreeMap<NamespaceId, CanonicalState>` with `get` returning `Option`. That means any caller that assumes core exists is relying on convention rather than the type system. The compiler cannot enforce “core namespace is always present”.

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-core/src/namespaced_state.rs:7` — `StoreState` is a plain map; core can be missing.

**Impact**
Structural invariant not encoded; subtle bugs when code assumes core exists (especially in daemon, repl, or query paths). This is the canonical namespace of the system; its absence is invalid.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Make core namespace presence structural.

1) Split core from non‑core:
- `struct StoreState { core: CanonicalState, other: BTreeMap<NamespaceId, CanonicalState> }`.
- `StoreState::new()` initializes `core` explicitly.

2) Prevent `NamespaceId::core()` from entering the `other` map:
- Introduce `NonCoreNamespaceId` wrapper returned by `NamespaceId::try_non_core()`.
- `StoreState::set_namespace_state` should accept `NonCoreNamespaceId` for `other` namespaces and a dedicated `set_core_state` for core.

3) Provide explicit core accessors:
- `fn core(&self) -> &CanonicalState`
- `fn core_mut(&mut self) -> &mut CanonicalState`

This makes “missing core” unrepresentable and pushes mistakes to compile time.
acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] `StoreState` cannot be constructed without a core namespace (compile‑time enforced).
- [ ] The `other` map cannot contain `NamespaceId::core()`.
- [ ] All call sites that need core use explicit `core()` accessors, not `get()`.
- [ ] Tests verify that non‑core namespace insertion rejects core.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
title: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
Observability: replace eprintln with tracing everywhere
description:
**Problem**
Mixed use of `tracing` and `eprintln!`. For a "daemon for swarms", logs matter.

**Design**
- Use `tracing` everywhere (esp sync start/stop, retries, backoff, durations, remote id)
- Add structured fields for key events
- Consider counters (sync failures, queue depth, refresh latency) - even structured logs help before full prometheus

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No `eprintln!` in non-test code
- [ ] Sync events have structured tracing spans
- [ ] Key metrics visible in logs (durations, retry counts)

**Files:** grep for eprintln, likely src/daemon/*.rs, src/git/*.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
WAL append fsync policy should follow realtime plan
description:
**Problem**
`SegmentWriter::append` currently uses `sync_all`, which fsyncs file metadata and directory on every append. The realtime plan specifies syncing file data per record and only syncing the directory when a new segment file is created/rotated. The current behavior is significantly slower and diverges from the intended design.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs
- src/daemon/wal/event_wal.rs
- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs (if metrics/limits need adjustments)
design:
Implement the plan’s fsync policy:
- Use `File::sync_data()` (or equivalent) for normal record appends.
- Only `sync_all()` when creating a new segment file or sealing/rotating a segment.
- Fsync the namespace directory when a new segment file path is created.
Update metrics to distinguish data fsync vs full fsync if needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Append path uses sync_data instead of sync_all.
- [ ] Directory fsync happens only on new segment creation/rotation.
- [ ] WAL tests updated to account for new sync behavior.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Split IPC into types/codec/client modules
description:
**Problem**
- `src/daemon/ipc.rs` is ~3k LOC and mixes protocol schema, codecs, socket path logic, and daemon auto-start client logic.
- This blurs boundaries and makes changes risky.

**Files:**
- src/daemon/ipc.rs
- src/daemon/mod.rs
- src/cli/mod.rs (imports)
- src/api/mod.rs (only if types move)
design:
- Convert `ipc.rs` into `src/daemon/ipc/` with `mod.rs`.
- Move request/response types into `ipc/types.rs`.
- Move encode/decode and error mapping into `ipc/codec.rs`.
- Move socket path + autostart + send_request/subscribe into `ipc/client.rs`.
- Re-export the same symbols from `ipc/mod.rs` to keep the public API stable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `src/daemon/ipc/` contains separate `types`, `codec`, and `client` modules.
- [ ] Existing imports continue to compile via re-exports.
- [ ] No behavior change; `cargo test` passes.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Detect and surface remote force-push/divergence
description:
**Problem**
If the remote history is rewritten (force-push), sync currently treats it like a normal remote update and proceeds. There's no explicit detection or warning to the user.

**Design**
Detect non-ancestor relationships between local/remote refs and surface a structured warning/error (e.g., status payload includes `diverged=true`). Consider requiring manual `bd sync --force` for history rewrites.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Divergence detection is explicit
- [ ] User-visible warning surfaced (status/IPC)
- [ ] Tests cover force-push/divergence scenario

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/api/mod.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Installer hardening: checksum verification + rate-limit robustness
description:
## What's Wrong
Install script fetches `releases/latest` unauthenticated and downloads a tarball without any integrity verification (checksum/signature). It may also fail due to GitHub API rate limits.

## Where
- scripts/install.sh: uses https://api.github.com/.../releases/latest and grep/sed parsing
- scripts/install.sh: downloads release asset and untars with no checksum

## Why It Matters
- Security: users execute a network script piping to bash; we should verify what we download.
- Reliability: rate limiting or JSON format changes can break installs.

## Suggested Fix
- Publish checksums (e.g., SHA256SUMS) as release assets and verify before install.
- Prefer GitHub release page redirect for latest tag, or allow `BD_VERSION` override.
- Use `jq` when available; fallback to robust parsing.

## Acceptance
- Installer verifies integrity of downloaded binary.
- Installer supports explicit version pinning.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Checkpoint snapshot builder rejects non-core namespaces
description: build_snapshot currently errors on non-core namespaces because StoreRuntime only has CanonicalState. Extend snapshot capture once StoreState is plumbed so checkpoint groups can include multiple namespaces.
darin@book
title: darin@book
Release scripts: make version bump portable across macOS/Linux
description:
## What's Wrong
`just release-patch` / `just release-minor` use `sed -i` in a way that's GNU-sed specific; BSD sed (macOS outside nix) requires an extension argument.

## Where
- justfile:67-68, 87-88

## Why It Matters
Contributors running release scripts on macOS without nix will hit a broken release flow.

## Suggested Fix
- Use a portable approach: `perl -pi -e`, or detect BSD vs GNU sed.
- Consider moving release logic to `scripts/release.sh` with explicit checks.

## Acceptance
- Release scripts work on macOS + Linux without requiring GNU sed.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Replace repl transport next-frame bools with enum
description: tests/integration/fixtures/repl_transport.rs uses drop_next/reorder_next bool flags. Replace with a typed enum that captures next-frame action (drop/reorder/normal) and keep delay as explicit state, so intent is encoded in types.
acceptance_criteria:
- drop_next/reorder_next bool fields removed
- next-frame action represented by enum
- tests in repl_transport updated
- cargo check, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo test pass

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Encode WAL RecordHeader invariants in types
description:
**Problem**
`RecordHeader` allows invalid combinations of flags/fields that are only rejected at encode/decode time. The compiler cannot prevent constructing headers where `request_sha256` is present without `client_request_id`, or other invalid flag/field pairings.

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/record.rs:65` (RecordHeader)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/record.rs:103` (encode-time validation)

**Impact**
WAL integrity depends on runtime checks. Any new constructor or refactor can accidentally build an invalid header that compiles and fails later.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Encode header invariants in the type system with an enum or structured subtype.

Option A (enum):
```rust
enum RecordHeader {
  NoClientRequest { /* fields */ },
  WithClientRequest { client_request_id: ClientRequestId, request_sha256: Option<[u8;32]>, /* fields */ }
}
```
- Encode/decode produce one of these variants.
- There is no state where `request_sha256` exists without `client_request_id`.

Option B (struct with nested request):
```rust
struct RecordHeader { common: CommonFields, request: Option<RequestMeta> }
struct RequestMeta { id: ClientRequestId, request_sha256: Option<[u8;32]> }
```
- Constructors enforce that `request_sha256` cannot exist without `RequestMeta`.

Make invalid combinations unrepresentable, not just validated at runtime.
acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] It is impossible to construct a header with `request_sha256` but no `client_request_id` (compile‑time enforced).
- [ ] Encode/decode APIs operate on the typed representation only.
- [ ] All call sites updated; no ad‑hoc header struct literals remain.
- [ ] Tests cover encode/decode round‑trip and reject invalid bytes at the boundary.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local

darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Store discovery e2e without BD_STORE_ID
description:
**Problem**
Multi-daemon tests force `BD_STORE_ID`, so we never validate real store discovery via remote URL canonicalization. That is the default deployment path.
design:
**Design**
- Add a ReplRig option to disable `BD_STORE_ID` env override.
- Build nodes by cloning the same remote and verify all daemons discover the same store_id from remotes.
- Add a slow e2e that creates issues on each node and asserts convergence + matching store_id.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ReplRig can run without `BD_STORE_ID`.
- [ ] Slow e2e validates store discovery and replication convergence.
- [ ] No writes outside ./tmp.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Crate boundary refactor: split beads-rs into workspace DAG
description:
**Problem**
The current single-crate architecture (`crates/beads-rs`) couples the public boundary (IPC protocol, schemas, client) with daemon internals. External consumers cannot use the IPC client without pulling in git/WAL/store implementation details.

**Goal**
Split into a dependency DAG:
- `beads-macros`: shared macro crate (enum_str!), no deps
- `beads-core`: pure domain + CRDT + error-code/payload types + Effect/Transience
- `beads-api`: canonical JSON schemas for IPC/--json
- `beads-surface`: IPC protocol + codec + client + request/patch/query schemas
- `beads-rs`: daemon + CLI + operational logic (re-exports for compatibility)

```
beads-macros
   ↑
beads-core
   ↑
beads-api
   ↑
beads-surface
   ↑
beads-rs
```

**Phases**
- Phase 0: Workspace scaffolding + versioning (bd-14fs.1)
- Phase 1A: Extract enum_str! macro (bd-14fs.11) ← NEW
- Phase 1: Extract core into beads-core (bd-14fs.2)
- Phase 2: Extract api into beads-api (bd-14fs.3)
- Phase 3A: Create beads-surface layout (bd-14fs.4)
- Phase 3B-D: Move IPC types, ops, query schemas (bd-14fs.5-7) ← PARALLEL
- Phase 3E: Refactor IPC codec (bd-14fs.8)
- Phase 3F: Move IPC client (bd-14fs.9)
- Phase 4: Re-wire beads-rs with re-export shims (bd-14fs.10)
- Cleanup: Add AGENTS.md documentation (bd-14fs.12)

**Key Gotchas** (from codebase analysis)
1. enum_str! used in 9 places (4 outside core) - beads-rs needs direct beads-macros dep
2. Core tests cannot depend on git/daemon
3. Query type duplication must be removed, not papered over
4. 2 From impls break orphan rules (OpError, IpcError → ErrorPayload)
5. 161 Response::err() call sites need explicit .to_error_payload()
6. Runtime + autostart overrides must move into surface
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All five crates compile independently
- [ ] beads-surface consumable without daemon internals
- [ ] Existing beads_rs::* paths still work via re-exports
- [ ] cargo test passes
- [ ] cargo clippy clean

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Phase 0: Workspace scaffolding and versioning
description:

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Phase 1A: Extract enum_str! macro to beads-macros
description:
**Problem**
bd-14fs.2 is too large - macro extraction should be a separate atomic step.

**Context from codebase analysis**
`enum_str!` is used in 9 places:
- `src/core/error.rs` (2 uses)
- `src/core/domain.rs` (2 uses)
- `src/core/wire_bead.rs` (1 use)
- `src/error.rs` (1 use) ← OUTSIDE core
- `src/cli/commands/store.rs` (1 use) ← OUTSIDE core
- `src/daemon/store/discovery.rs` (1 use) ← OUTSIDE core
- `src/daemon/repl/proto.rs` (1 use) ← OUTSIDE core

**Implication**: beads-rs needs direct dependency on beads-macros, not just via beads-core.

**Changes**

1. **Create macro in beads-macros**:
   ```rust
   // crates/beads-macros/src/lib.rs
   #[macro_export]
   macro_rules! enum_str { ... }
   ```

2. **Update beads-rs to use it**:
   - Add `beads-macros` dependency to Cargo.toml
   - Replace `#[macro_use] mod enum_str;` with `pub use beads_macros::enum_str;`
   - Delete `crates/beads-rs/src/enum_str.rs`

3. **All existing `crate::enum_str!` calls keep working** because the macro is re-exported at crate root.

**Files**
- crates/beads-macros/src/lib.rs (populate with macro)
- crates/beads-rs/Cargo.toml (add beads-macros dep)
- crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs (re-export macro)
- crates/beads-rs/src/enum_str.rs (delete)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-macros` passes
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-rs` passes
- [ ] All 9 enum_str! call sites still work
- [ ] enum_str.rs deleted from beads-rs

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Add AGENTS.md files for new crates
description:
**Problem**
New crates (beads-macros, beads-core, beads-api, beads-surface) need AGENTS.md files documenting their boundaries, following the pattern established in existing crate directories.

**Files to create**
- `crates/beads-macros/AGENTS.md`
- `crates/beads-core/src/AGENTS.md` (can be moved from beads-rs/src/core/AGENTS.md)
- `crates/beads-api/src/AGENTS.md` (can be moved from beads-rs/src/api/AGENTS.md)
- `crates/beads-surface/src/AGENTS.md` (new)

**Content pattern** (from existing AGENTS.md files):
- Boundary: what this directory defines
- Depends on: upstream dependencies
- Depended on by: downstream consumers
- NEVER: anti-patterns to avoid
- How to work here: guidelines
- Verification: commands to run
- Dont copy this: things to avoid

**Surface crate AGENTS.md should include**:
- Boundary: IPC protocol types, schemas, client (public surface boundary)
- Depends on: beads-core, beads-api
- Depended on by: beads-rs daemon, external clients
- NEVER: include daemon operational logic, git/WAL/store internals
- Verification: cargo test, cargo check
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] beads-macros has AGENTS.md
- [ ] beads-core has AGENTS.md
- [ ] beads-api has AGENTS.md
- [ ] beads-surface has AGENTS.md
- [ ] Each documents boundary, deps, and verification

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Phase 1: Extract core into beads-core
description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/src/core/**` needs to become standalone `beads-core` crate. Currently core has dependencies on:
- `crate::error::{Effect, Transience}` (must move into core)
- Tests in `core/state.rs` call `crate::git::wire::serialize_*` (must move tests out)

Note: enum_str! macro extraction is handled separately in bd-14fs.11.

**Changes**

1. **Move Effect + Transience into beads-core**:
   - Define `beads_core::Effect` and `beads_core::Transience` (in `core/effect.rs` or `core/error.rs`)
   - Update `core/error.rs` to use local types instead of `crate::error::{Effect, Transience}`
   - In beads-rs: `pub use beads_core::{Effect, Transience};`
   - Update `crates/beads-rs/src/error.rs` to use `beads_core::{Effect, Transience}`

2. **Move directory**: `crates/beads-rs/src/core/**` → `crates/beads-core/src/**`
   - `crates/beads-core/src/lib.rs` mirrors old `core/mod.rs` structure
   - Keep `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`

3. **Update beads-rs to re-export**:
   - Replace `pub mod core;` with `pub use beads_core as core;`
   - This preserves `beads_rs::core::BeadId` etc.

4. **Move git-dependent tests out of core**:
   - `core/state.rs` tests that call `crate::git::wire::serialize_*` → move to `crates/beads-rs/tests/core_state_fingerprint.rs`
   - Rule: **no beads-core tests depend on daemon/git/IPC modules**

5. **Update Cargo.toml files**:
   - `beads-core/Cargo.toml`: add `beads-macros` dependency, copy relevant deps from beads-rs
   - `beads-rs/Cargo.toml`: add `beads-core` dependency

**Files**
- crates/beads-core/src/** (moved from beads-rs/src/core/**)
- crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs (re-export as core)
- crates/beads-rs/src/error.rs (use beads_core types)
- crates/beads-rs/tests/core_state_fingerprint.rs (new - moved tests)
- crates/beads-core/Cargo.toml
- crates/beads-rs/Cargo.toml
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-core` passes (core builds in isolation)
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-rs` passes
- [ ] beads-core has ZERO dependencies on beads-rs modules
- [ ] `beads_rs::core::BeadId` still resolves
- [ ] All tests pass

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Phase 2: Extract api into beads-api
description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/src/api/**` needs to become standalone `beads-api` crate. Currently there's type duplication:
- `api/` has schema types (Issue, IssueSummary, QueryResult, etc.)
- `daemon/query_model.rs` has duplicate internal types
- `api/convert.rs` converts between them

**Decision**: Daemon should use beads-api types directly. Delete duplicates and conversion layer.

**Changes**

1. **Move directory**: `crates/beads-rs/src/api/**` → `crates/beads-api/src/**`

2. **Update imports in moved files**:
   - `use crate::core::...` → `use beads_core::...`

3. **Delete conversion layer**:
   - Remove `crates/beads-api/src/convert.rs` (was `api/convert.rs`)
   - Delete `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/query_model.rs`

4. **Update beads-rs to re-export**:
   - Replace `pub mod api;` with `pub use beads_api as api;`
   - This preserves `beads_rs::api::QueryResult`

5. **Update daemon to use beads-api directly**:
   - `daemon/query_executor.rs`: change imports from `daemon::query_model::*` to `beads_api::*`
   - `daemon/admin.rs`: return `beads_api::QueryResult` variants directly
   - `daemon/core.rs`: use `beads_api::*` types
   - Use existing constructors like `Issue::from_view(...)`, `IssueSummary::from_view(...)`

6. **Update Cargo.toml**:
   - `beads-api/Cargo.toml`: depends on `beads-core` and serde
   - `beads-rs/Cargo.toml`: add `beads-api` dependency

**Key insight**: Keep beads-api as schemas + conversions from core types only (e.g., `impl From<&core::Note> for api::Note`). This avoids orphan rules.

**Files**
- crates/beads-api/src/** (moved from beads-rs/src/api/**)
- crates/beads-api/src/convert.rs (delete)
- crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs (re-export as api)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/query_model.rs (delete)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/query_executor.rs (update imports)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/admin.rs (update imports)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/core.rs (update imports)
- crates/beads-api/Cargo.toml
- crates/beads-rs/Cargo.toml
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-api` passes (api builds in isolation)
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-rs` passes
- [ ] No query_model.rs exists in daemon
- [ ] No convert.rs exists in api
- [ ] `beads_rs::api::QueryResult` still resolves
- [ ] Daemon constructs beads_api types directly
- [ ] All tests pass

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Phase 3A: Create beads-surface crate layout
description:
**Problem**
Need the beads-surface crate structure before moving IPC/ops/query types.

**Changes**
Create the crate layout:

```
crates/beads-surface/
├── Cargo.toml
└── src/
    ├── lib.rs
    ├── ipc/
    │   ├── mod.rs
    │   ├── types.rs    (Request, Response, ResponsePayload, etc.)
    │   ├── codec.rs    (NDJSON framing, encode/decode)
    │   └── client.rs   (IpcClient)
    ├── ops.rs          (Patch, BeadPatch, OpResult)
    └── query.rs        (Filters, SortField)
```

**Cargo.toml dependencies**:
- beads-core
- beads-api
- serde, serde_json, thiserror
- nix (signals/user)
- tracing
- optionally tempfile for tests

**lib.rs public API**:
```rust
pub mod ipc;
pub mod ops;
pub mod query;

pub use ipc::{Request, Response, ResponsePayload, IpcClient, IpcError, ...};
pub use ops::{Patch, BeadPatch, OpResult};
pub use query::{Filters, SortField};
```

**Files**
- crates/beads-surface/Cargo.toml (new)
- crates/beads-surface/src/lib.rs (new)
- crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/mod.rs (new, empty stubs)
- crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/types.rs (new, empty)
- crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/codec.rs (new, empty)
- crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/client.rs (new, empty)

- crates/beads-surface/src/query.rs (new, empty)

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Phase 3D: Extract query filter schemas to beads-surface
description:
**Problem**
`Filters` and `SortField` are embedded in IPC `Request::List` and other requests - they're part of the public protocol boundary.

**What's in daemon/query.rs today**:
- `Filters` - query filter struct (boundary)
- `SortField` - sort specification (boundary)
- `Filters::matches(&BeadView)` - filter semantics (boundary - part of protocol contract)
- Internal query execution logic (daemon-only)

**Decision**: Keep `Filters::matches` in surface because filter semantics are part of the public protocol contract.

**Changes**

1. **Create beads-surface/src/query.rs** with filter schemas:
   ```rust
   use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
   use beads_core::BeadView;

   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   pub struct Filters { ... }

   impl Filters {
       /// Returns true if the bead matches all specified filters.
       /// This is part of the protocol contract - filter semantics must be consistent.
       pub fn matches(&self, bead: &BeadView) -> bool { ... }
   }

   #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   pub enum SortField { ... }
   ```

2. **Keep daemon-only query execution** in beads-rs:
   - Import: `use beads_surface::query::{Filters, SortField};`
   - Keep internal execution logic that iterates over state

3. **Remove duplicates from daemon/query.rs**:
   - Delete `Filters` and `SortField` definitions
   - Import from beads-surface instead

**Files**
- crates/beads-surface/src/query.rs (new - filter types + matches())
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/query.rs (refactored - execution only)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Filters and SortField defined in beads-surface
- [ ] Filters::matches() implemented in beads-surface
- [ ] Filter semantics testable at boundary level
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-surface` passes
- [ ] daemon/query.rs imports from beads-surface

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Phase 3E: Refactor IPC codec into surface + daemon error mapping
description:
**Problem**
`daemon/ipc/codec.rs` does two things:
1. NDJSON framing + JSON encode/decode (belongs in surface)
2. Mapping daemon errors to ErrorPayload (belongs in daemon)

**Orphan rule issue**:
Currently `impl From<OpError> for ErrorPayload` exists. After extraction, ErrorPayload is in beads-core (foreign), OpError is in beads-rs, so this `From` impl becomes illegal.

**Affected impls** (daemon/ipc/codec.rs):
- Line 23: `impl From<OpError> for ErrorPayload`
- Line 879: `impl From<IpcError> for ErrorPayload`

**Call sites**: 161 uses of `Response::err(e)` where `e: OpError` relies on implicit conversion.

**Recommended fix** (minimal changes):
1. Add `IntoErrorPayload` trait in beads-rs:
   ```rust
   pub trait IntoErrorPayload {
       fn into_error_payload(self) -> ErrorPayload;
   }
   impl IntoErrorPayload for OpError { ... }
   ```
2. Add extension method on Response in beads-rs:
   ```rust
   pub trait ResponseExt {
       fn err_from<E: IntoErrorPayload>(e: E) -> Response;
   }
   ```
3. Find-replace: `Response::err(` → `Response::err_from(` in daemon code

This keeps surface clean (no `impl Into` bounds) and changes are mechanical.

**Changes**

1. **Move framing code to beads-surface/src/ipc/codec.rs**:
   - `IpcError` (parse/io/frame-too-large/version mismatch)
   - `encode_response`, `send_response`, `decode_request_with_limits`, `read_requests`

2. **Create daemon error mapping** `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/ipc/error_mapping.rs`:
   - `IntoErrorPayload` trait
   - `impl IntoErrorPayload for OpError`
   - `impl IntoErrorPayload for IpcError`
   - `ResponseExt` trait for `Response::err_from()`

3. **Mechanical find-replace** in daemon code:
   `Response::err(` → `Response::err_from(`

**Files**
- crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/codec.rs (framing only)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/ipc/error_mapping.rs (new)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/ipc/mod.rs
- Daemon files (mechanical find-replace)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] IpcError defined in beads-surface
- [ ] encode/decode/framing functions in beads-surface
- [ ] No impl From<DaemonError> for ErrorPayload (orphan rule)
- [ ] All 161 error conversions use explicit .to_error_payload()
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-surface` passes
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-rs` passes

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Stateright repl: network semantics + history hooks
description:
**Problem**
Even with ActorModel, we’re not yet leveraging Stateright’s network semantics selection, message history capture, or ordered-reliable-link adapter to control assumptions and reduce state.

**Goal**
Extend the repl ActorModel to fully use Stateright’s network model and history hooks.
design:
**Design**
1) Add CLI/config switches in the model example to choose:
   - `Network::UnorderedDuplicating`
   - `Network::UnorderedNonDuplicating`
   - `Network::Ordered`
   - `LossyNetwork::Yes/No`
2) Implement `record_msg_in` / `record_msg_out` to collect:
   - last delivered sequence per (ns, origin)
   - in-flight want ranges
   - ack histories
3) Add an ordered-link mode using `actor::ordered_reliable_link::ActorWrapper` to model a reliable channel between replicas when desired.
4) Add properties that use history (not just actor state) to assert:
   - ACK monotonicity even under loss/dup
   - WANTs do not skip gaps
   - equivalence between delivered history and applied watermark

**References**
- `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/src/actor/network.rs`
- `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/src/actor/ordered_reliable_link.rs`
- `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/examples/timers.rs`
acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] Repl model supports multiple network semantics via CLI flags.
- [ ] History hooks are used for at least one property involving message-level invariants.
- [ ] Ordered reliable link mode exists and can be toggled in the example.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Decouple StoreState from RepoState (rename RepoState)
description:
**Problem**
- `RepoState` owns canonical `StoreState` plus git lane metadata, mixing concerns.
- `StoreRuntime` holds `repo_state`, so store state is indirectly coupled to git operations.
- `RepoState` name conflicts with `src/repo.rs` and is easy to open by mistake.

**Files:**
- src/daemon/repo.rs
- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
- src/daemon/core.rs
- src/git/sync.rs (only if types are referenced)
design:
- Move `StoreState` ownership into `StoreRuntime` (`state: StoreState`).
- Rename `RepoState` to `GitLaneState` (or `CheckpointLaneState`) and keep only git/checkpoint metadata there.
- Update `Daemon` maps to store `GitLaneState` separately from store runtime.
- Remove unused fields like `wal_sequence` if they are now owned by WAL.
- Update all references/imports.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canonical store state lives in `StoreRuntime`, not `RepoState`.
- [ ] Git lane metadata lives in `GitLaneState` (renamed file/module).
- [ ] No name collision with `src/repo.rs`.
- [ ] Tests pass.

Multi-process replication e2e harness

darin@book
title: darin@book
IPC: unit ResponsePayload variants serialize as null (ambiguous)
description:
**Problem**
`ResponsePayload` is `#[serde(untagged)]` and includes multiple unit variants (`Synced`, `Initialized`, `Pong`, `ShuttingDown`). These serialize to JSON `null`, so clients can deserialize ambiguously.

Example symptom: `bd init` prints `synced` instead of `initialized`.

**Design**
Switch `ResponsePayload` to an explicit tagged representation (e.g. `#[serde(tag="type", content="data", rename_all="snake_case")]`), or ensure unit variants serialize as distinct strings/objects. Keep backward compatibility via versioning or dual-decode.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd init` prints `initialized` on success
- [ ] Unit ok payloads are not serialized as `null`
- [ ] JSON decoding is deterministic
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** `src/daemon/ipc.rs`, `src/cli/render.rs`

title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Ordered-link repl model can't inspect wrapped state
description: In repl_core_machine ordered-link mode, ActorWrapper uses StateWrapper with private wrapped_state, so we can't apply node-level invariants. Consider adding a local adapter or upstream accessor to expose the inner state (or expand history-based checks) so ordered-link runs the full property suite.
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
complete_sync must not drop local mutations on join failure
description:
**Problem**
In `Daemon::complete_sync`, if mutations happened during sync and `CanonicalState::join` fails, the code discards local state and keeps `synced_state`. That can drop mutations made during the sync window.

**Design**
When join fails, detect/resolution collisions (reuse `git::collision` logic) and retry the merge. If merge still fails, keep local state, mark dirty, and surface/log the failure so operators can intervene.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Local mutations are never dropped on join failure
- [ ] Collision resolution is applied for dirty-after-sync merges
- [ ] Tests cover the join-failure path
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/git/collision.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
IPC per-request Ping adds ~60ms overhead to each CLI request
description:
**Problem**
Every IPC request does a Ping (version check) round-trip before the actual request.

- Direct socket: ~60ms for one request
- CLI: ~120ms+ for one request (Ping + actual)
- `bd show` (human mode): 8 requests × 60ms ping = ~480ms overhead

**Evidence**
```
bd list -n 1: 64ms    (1 actual request + 1 ping)
bd --json show: 26ms  (2 actual + 2 pings? or cached?)
bd show simple: 480ms (8 requests, each with ping)
```

**Design Options**
1. Connection reuse: Keep single connection per CLI invocation, verify once
2. Session tokens: First Ping returns token, skip verification for subsequent
3. Request batching: Combine Show+Deps+Notes into ShowWithContext

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd show simple-bead` < 200ms
- [ ] No version mismatch regressions
closed_reason: Skip version ping when daemon.meta.json matches; added meta-based version checks and unit tests for match/mismatch.

darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Checkpoint dirty shards should be typed (no raw String paths)
description:
**Problem**
`StoreRuntime` tracks checkpoint dirty shards as `BTreeSet<String>` of shard paths (`checkpoint_dirty_shards` / `checkpoint_dirty_inflight`). This mixes path construction with storage and makes it easy to emit inconsistent shard paths or forget to update a path format. It also spreads stringly-typed shard logic across the runtime.

**Files**
- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
- src/daemon/checkpoint_snapshot.rs (or snapshot builder)
- src/daemon/checkpoint.rs helpers (shard_path)
design: Introduce a typed `CheckpointShardPath` (or `CheckpointShardKey` + `to_path()`), containing namespace, shard kind, and shard id. Store these in the dirty shard sets instead of raw strings, and only render to path strings when serializing snapshots. Update helpers like `shard_path` to construct the typed value.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Dirty shard tracking uses a typed shard/path type, not `String`.
- [ ] Snapshot serialization still emits the same on-disk shard paths.
- [ ] Dirty shard tests updated to use the typed value.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
DurabilityCoordinator wait blocks daemon loop; refactor to async waiters
description:
**Problem**
DurabilityCoordinator waits by sleeping in the daemon state thread, blocking other IPC/git handling during ReplicatedFsync.

**Design**
Move durability waits into async waiters managed by run_state_loop. After local apply, register a waiter with the response channel and poll ack status on a short tick or ack notifications; respond when satisfied or timeout without blocking the loop.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] ReplicatedFsync requests no longer block the state loop.
- [ ] DurabilityTimeout still returns retryable error with receipt.
- [ ] Tests cover waiter completion + timeout paths.

**Files:** src/daemon/server.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/durability_coordinator.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
Nest admin Request variants under Request::Admin(AdminOp)
description:
**Problem**
The Request enum in src/daemon/ipc/types.rs has 11 admin-related variants at the top level:
- AdminStatus
- AdminMetrics  
- AdminDoctor
- AdminScrub
- AdminFlush
- AdminCheckpointWait
- AdminFingerprint
- AdminReloadPolicies
- AdminReloadLimits
- AdminReloadReplication
- AdminRotateReplicaId
- AdminMaintenanceMode
- AdminRebuildIndex

This adds cognitive load when pattern matching (47 total variants) and makes the Request enum harder to navigate. Admin operations are a cohesive group that should be nested.

**Design**
1. Create `AdminOp` enum with all admin variants (without Admin prefix):

```rust
// src/daemon/ipc/types.rs

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(tag = "admin_op", rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum AdminOp {
    Status { repo: PathBuf },
    Metrics { repo: PathBuf },
    Doctor { repo: PathBuf, fix: bool },
    Scrub { repo: PathBuf, fix: bool },
    Flush { repo: PathBuf },
    CheckpointWait { repo: PathBuf, timeout_ms: Option<u64> },
    Fingerprint { repo: PathBuf, mode: AdminFingerprintMode, sample: AdminFingerprintSample },
    ReloadPolicies { repo: PathBuf },
    ReloadLimits { repo: PathBuf },
    ReloadReplication { repo: PathBuf },
    RotateReplicaId { repo: PathBuf },
    MaintenanceMode { repo: PathBuf, enabled: bool },
    RebuildIndex { repo: PathBuf },
}
```

2. Replace 11 Request variants with one:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(tag = "op", rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum Request {
    // ... other variants
    Admin(AdminOp),  // Replaces 11 AdminX variants
}
```

3. Update dispatch in coord.rs:

```rust
Request::Admin(op) => self.handle_admin(op),
```

4. Update CLI admin.rs handler to construct AdminOp variants

**Design Notes**
- Wire format changes: `{"op": "admin_status", ...}` → `{"op": "admin", "admin_op": "status", ...}`
- This is a breaking protocol change — requires IPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION bump to 3
- Can be done after IPC documentation bead to have clear migration notes

**Acceptance**
- [ ] AdminOp enum created with all 11 variants
- [ ] Request enum has single Admin(AdminOp) variant
- [ ] IPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION bumped to 3
- [ ] coord.rs dispatch updated
- [ ] src/cli/commands/admin.rs updated to construct AdminOp
- [ ] Wire format documented in IPC_PROTOCOL.md
- [ ] cargo clippy passes
- [ ] cargo test passes
- [ ] Integration tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc/types.rs, src/daemon/coord.rs, src/cli/commands/admin.rs, docs/IPC_PROTOCOL.md

title: Replication does not enforce namespace allowlists
description:
**Problem**
Inbound replication accepts EVENTS for any namespace, regardless of negotiated namespaces. Outbound replication continues to publish events for namespaces the peer did not accept. This breaks namespace policy invariants and makes the allowlist in HELLO/WELCOME advisory only.

**Evidence**
- src/daemon/repl/session.rs: handle_events() never checks frame.eid.namespace against peer.incoming_namespaces (stored on SessionPeer).
- src/daemon/repl/manager.rs: run_peer_loop() filters outgoing events using offered_set (plan/offered), not the negotiated accepted_namespaces from session.peer().
- src/daemon/repl/manager.rs: handle_want() has no allowed_set filter, so it can send events for namespaces the peer never accepted.

**Why this hurts**
Namespace policy is meant to be a hard boundary. Without enforcement, a misconfigured or malicious peer can ingest or receive data outside its allowed namespaces.
design:
**Design**
1) Enforce inbound namespaces in Session::handle_events(): if namespace not in peer.incoming_namespaces, return an error payload (NamespacePolicyViolation or InvalidRequest) and close.
2) Outbound: after handshake, derive accepted_set from session.peer().accepted_namespaces and use it for filtering pending events + hot cache + live stream. Replace offered_set usage.
3) Update manager::handle_want() to accept an allowed_set (like server.rs) and ignore or error on wants outside accepted_set.
4) Add tests for both directions (reject unexpected namespace; only send accepted namespaces).

Note: inbound server already builds accepted_set; align outbound to use the negotiated set.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Inbound session rejects EVENTS for namespaces not in incoming_namespaces.
- [ ] Outbound streaming/hot cache only sends namespaces in accepted_namespaces.
- [ ] Outbound WANT handling is scoped to accepted_namespaces.
- [ ] Tests cover accept/reject behavior.

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/session.rs, src/daemon/repl/manager.rs, src/daemon/repl/server.rs

description: darin@book

Reduce repl_e2e node count/timeouts where 2 nodes suffice
description: tests/integration/daemon/repl_e2e.rs often uses 3 nodes + long timeouts even when 2 would cover the scenario. Audit tests and drop to 2 nodes where quorum isn't needed, and tighten timeouts based on fast-test settings.
darin@book
title: darin@book
mutation: claimed bead must imply in_progress
description:
Problem
- plan_create sets assignee/assignee_expires but does not set workflow status.
- This can create a bead that is claimed but still Workflow::Open.
- Elsewhere (migration, claim ops) imply claimed => in_progress, but not enforced.

Impact
- Incorrect status semantics; readiness/filters can misbehave.
- Invariant exists in developer lore, not in types.
design:
Design
- Encode the invariant in one place:
  - If assignee is set at creation, automatically set status=InProgress (and clear closed_reason/on_branch).
  - Alternatively, make Claim+Status a combined patch type or a validation rule in ValidatedBeadPatch: claim set requires status=in_progress when current status is open.
- Prefer type-level coupling (e.g., ClaimState::Claimed implies WorkflowStatus::InProgress) in the mutation planning layer.

Scatter fit
- Make the invariant explicit; no caller can create claimed+open state.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- crates/beads-core/src/event.rs (if validation rule added)
- crates/beads-core/src/apply.rs (if apply enforces)
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- Creating a bead with assignee always results in workflow=in_progress.
- It is impossible to construct a mutation that yields claimed+open (compile-time or runtime validation).
- Tests cover create-with-assignee sets status and rejects invalid combinations.

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Type-safe daemon: eliminate invariant-checking unwraps
description:
Eliminate unwraps in daemon code by encoding proven invariants in the type system.

**Goal:** No `unwrap()` in non-test daemon paths where failures can be caused by repo state or git IO.

**Approach:** "Parse, dont validate" — move checks to where data enters the system and let types remember what has been validated.

**Subtasks:**
- Serde boundary validation for identity types
- Encapsulate DepKey (no self-loops)
- LoadedRemote proof type
- require_live helper
- with_mutation transaction helper
- Collapse live/tombstones into BeadEntry
- Remove duplicate key from DepEdge

See individual beads for design details.
closed_reason: Core goal achieved: eliminated all .unwrap() calls on bead lookups in daemon paths. Completed: serde boundary validation, DepKey encapsulation, LoadedRemote proof type, require_live helpers + refactoring, with_mutation helper. Remaining low-priority cleanup (bd-mgf, bd-di1) filed as separate beads.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Update stateright models for REALTIME_PLAN v0.5
description:
**Problem**
Stateright models in `beads_stateright_models` still reflect v0.4 realtime spec (self-hashing envelopes, unknown-key preservation, GC marker events, PENDING/COMMITTED idempotency). REALTIME_PLAN.md v0.5 simplified hashing, removed unknown-key preservation and GitCheckpointed durability, and deferred GC markers. Models should match v0.5.

**Design**
- Update `beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs` to v0.5: hash over EventBody bytes, sha/prev in headers, no unknown-key preservation, no NamespaceGcMarker, no GitCheckpointed.
- Update toy hashing helpers (`beads_stateright_models/src/toy_codec.rs`) and models (`beads_stateright_models/examples/hash_prev_machine.rs`, `beads_stateright_models/examples/canonical_hash_machine.rs`) to use v0.5 hash/prev semantics.
- Update idempotency model (`beads_stateright_models/examples/idempotency_receipt_machine.rs`) to use stable idempotency mapping (no PENDING/COMMITTED receipts).
- Remove or mark GC floor model as deferred in v0.5.
- Refresh doc comments/README/todo in the models crate to reference v0.5 sections.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] All models compile under v0.5 assumptions (no v0.4-only features).
- [ ] Hash/prev models verify sha over EventBody bytes and use header prev_sha semantics.
- [ ] Idempotency model reflects stable request mapping (no pending/committed states).
- [ ] GC floor example is removed or explicitly marked deferred for v0.5.

darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Use typed internal errors in replication (avoid ErrorPayload in core logic)
description:
**Problem**
Replication internals use ErrorPayload as their primary error type (SessionResult = Result<T, Box<ErrorPayload>> in repl/session.rs, and ReplSessionStore/ingest paths). This pushes boundary formatting into core logic, loses structured error intent, and conflicts with error_design guidance (use domain errors internally, map at boundaries).

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/session.rs, src/daemon/repl/runtime.rs, src/daemon/repl/manager.rs, src/daemon/repl/server.rs
design:
**Design**
- Introduce a ReplError enum capturing internal error cases with structured fields.
- Convert ReplError -> ErrorPayload only at the protocol boundary (SessionAction::Close / outbound ERROR frames, IPC responses).
- Update SessionStore trait and ReplIngestRequest to use ReplError instead of Box<ErrorPayload>.
- Add conversion tests to ensure payload formatting stays stable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Replication core logic no longer returns ErrorPayload directly.
- [ ] Boundary adapters produce ErrorPayload from ReplError.
- [ ] Tests cover ReplError -> ErrorPayload mapping.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Expose namespace in Issue and IssueSummary API
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN requires namespace exposure in issue summaries and issue views. API types Issue/IssueSummary lack namespace, so clients can’t distinguish multi-namespace data.

**Design**
Add `namespace` field (NamespaceId or string) to Issue and IssueSummary in src/api/mod.rs. Update Issue::from_bead and IssueSummary::from_bead to take namespace context (or add new constructors) and update query executor call sites to pass the read namespace. Update IssueSummary::from_issue to copy namespace.

**Files**
- src/api/mod.rs
- src/daemon/query_executor.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Issue and IssueSummary include namespace in JSON output
- [ ] All query results populate namespace based on read namespace
- [ ] Existing query tests updated for new field

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Note ID collisions should be guarded
description:
**Problem**
Note IDs are random 8-char base58; collisions are unlikely but possible. A collision overwrites an existing note in the CRDT map.

**Design**
Include actor + timestamp in note IDs or retry generation until unique in-bead. Add a test that forces collision handling.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Note ID generation is collision-safe
- [ ] Tests cover collision path

**Files:** src/core/identity.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs

Add bd dep cycles detection command
description:
beads-go has 'bd dep cycles' to detect circular dependencies in the dependency graph.

Files: src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/dep.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
LoadGenerator use persistent IpcConnection to avoid per-request connect
description: tests/integration/fixtures/load_gen.rs currently opens a new IPC connection per request. Use IpcConnection (or a small per-worker connection pool) so large loads don't pay connect/handshake each time.
darin@book
title: darin@book
Remote URL canonicalization + type tightening
description:
**Problem**
Remote URL normalization is too loose (ssh/https variants, ports, users). This can split canonical state across clones and cause data divergence.

**Design**
Add a stricter RemoteUrl parser that:
- strips scheme/user, lowercases host
- normalizes ssh scp-style and ssh:// URLs
- preserves port when present (host:port)
- trims .git and trailing slashes
Add focused tests for common variants (ssh/https/ssh://, with/without user, with port).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Same remote in different URL forms maps to identical RemoteUrl
- [ ] Tests cover common cases + edge cases
- [ ] No regressions for file:// or local paths

**Files:** src/daemon/remote.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Sync fetch must not clobber local ref (ancestry check + backup)
description:
**Problem**
`SyncProcess::fetch` force-updates `refs/heads/beads/store` to match remote and uses `unwrap_or(Oid::zero())` for ref lookup. If local is ahead/diverged, this overwrites local commits; if ref lookup errors, we treat it as missing. Both can lose durable local history.

**Design**
- Use ref lookup that distinguishes NotFound vs error.
- Only fast-forward local ref when it is an ancestor of remote.
- If local is ahead/diverged, create a backup ref (e.g., `refs/beads/backup/<oid>`) before any update and avoid forcing local to remote.
- Keep commit parent as remote head for linear history.
- Add tests covering local-ahead and diverged scenarios.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No forced local ref update when local ahead/diverged
- [ ] Backup refs created for diverged local history
- [ ] Ref lookup errors are surfaced
- [ ] Tests cover ancestry check + backup behavior
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/git/error.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
checkpoint shards: type the paths and enforce canonical order
description:
Problem
- CheckpointSnapshot/CheckpointManifest store shard paths as raw String and dirty_shards as BTreeSet<String>.
- Path validation is scattered (layout::parse_shard_path, import::validate_rel_path, publish tree builder).
- Shard contents accept duplicates/unsorted lines; insert_live silently overwrites duplicates during import.

Impact
- A typo in dirty_shards can delete or skip unrelated files during incremental export.
- Malformed shards can silently drop data (last write wins) instead of failing fast.
- Violates parse-don't-validate and Scatter (path invariants live in multiple places).
design:
Design
- Introduce validated newtypes: ShardName ("[0-9a-f]{2}.jsonl"), CheckpointShardPath { namespace, kind, shard: ShardName }.
- Use CheckpointShardPath (not String) in CheckpointSnapshot.shards and dirty_shards; derive the string path only at the IO boundary.
- Change CheckpointManifest.files to BTreeMap<CheckpointShardPath, ManifestFile> (or a thin CheckpointFilePath newtype).
- Enforce dirty_shards ⊆ shards at construction (return error if not).
- In import_checkpoint, add canonical validation for shard content:
  - state shards: strictly increasing BeadId, no duplicates.
  - tombstones: strictly increasing BeadId (or TombstoneKey) with no duplicates.
  - deps: strictly increasing DepKey, no duplicates.
  - notes: strictly increasing (bead_id, note_id) with no duplicates.
- Keep ordering guarantees in export (already sorted) and assert in tests.

Scatter fit
- Centralize path parsing/validation in one type; callers only handle typed paths.
- Canonical ordering checks live in one parser per shard kind.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/layout.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/types.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/manifest.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/export.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/import.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/publish.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/checkpoint/cache.rs
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- Snapshot and manifest types use typed shard paths; invalid paths cannot be constructed.
- build_snapshot/build_snapshot_from_state reject dirty_shards outside of shard set.
- import_checkpoint rejects duplicate or out-of-order entries in state/tombstone/dep/note shards.
- Tests cover path validation, dirty_shards subset enforcement, and duplicate-entry rejection.

title: Encode EventFrame prev/seq invariants in types
description:
**Problem**
`EventFrameV1` allows impossible combinations of `origin_seq` and `prev_sha256` (e.g., seq=1 with prev set), and these are rejected only at runtime in `verify_event_frame`. This is a structural invariant that should be expressed in the type system so callers can’t construct invalid frames.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs:2500` — runtime prev/seq checks in `verify_event_frame`.
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs:150` — `EventFrameV1` definition.

Severity: low‑level framing invariants are enforced only by convention; easy to break when creating new frame sources.
design:
**Design**
Add typestated frame variants to encode the prev/seq relation.

Option A (sum types):
- `enum EventFrame { Genesis(GenesisFrame), WithPrev(NonGenesisFrame) }`.
- `GenesisFrame` has `origin_seq = Seq1::from_u64(1)` and `prev_sha256 = None`.
- `NonGenesisFrame` carries `origin_seq > 1` and `prev_sha256 = Some`.

Option B (phantom state):
- `EventFrameV1<HasPrev>` with `HasPrev = PrevRequired | PrevForbidden` and constructors enforcing seq rules.

Migration:
- Keep wire parsing into raw struct, then convert into typed frame with a fallible conversion.
- Update `verify_event_frame` to accept only typed frame; runtime prev/seq checks become unreachable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Construction of a frame with seq=1 and prev set (or seq>1 without prev) is impossible without a fallible conversion.
- [ ] `verify_event_frame` takes typed frame and no longer does prev/seq validation internally.
- [ ] Existing framing sources updated to use constructors; tests updated accordingly.

title: Replication ingest must require a contiguous verified batch type
description:
**Problem**
`SessionStore::ingest_remote_batch` accepts a raw `Vec<VerifiedEvent<PrevVerified>>`. Nothing in the type system guarantees: (a) same namespace + origin, (b) strictly increasing contiguous `origin_seq`, (c) no duplicates, (d) prev_sha chain consistency. Today the batch is sourced from `GapBuffer` so it is usually valid, but a single future refactor (or a new caller) can pass an invalid batch and we will append to WAL + apply state before later checks fail. That can produce WAL/state divergence and incorrect watermarks.

This violates parse-don’t-validate (we validate in scattered places) and keeps correctness implicit.

**Impact**
- Safety bug: WAL written with non-contiguous or duplicated events; state/apply diverges from WAL.
- Hard to localize: failures surface far from where the batch was constructed.
- Compiler cannot prevent misuse.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs` (batch construction)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/store.rs` / `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/runtime.rs` (SessionStore APIs)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/core.rs` (ingest_remote_batch)
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Make contiguity unrepresentable as a runtime-only property. Encode it in a type that is the *only* input to ingest.

1) Introduce `ContiguousBatch` (name bikesheddable) in core or repl module:
```rust
pub struct ContiguousBatch {
  namespace: NamespaceId,
  origin: ReplicaId,
  first: Seq1,
  last: Seq1,
  events: NonEmptyVec<VerifiedEvent<PrevVerified>>,
}
```
Invariants enforced in constructor:
- all events share namespace + origin
- `origin_seq` strictly increasing and contiguous
- no duplicates
- prev_sha chain matches event order
- `first`/`last` match bounds

2) Construction should be *parse boundary only*:
- `GapBuffer::ingest_one` / `drain_ready` should return `ContiguousBatch` instead of `Vec<VerifiedEvent<PrevVerified>>`.
- WAL backfill (if used for repl ingest) should also construct `ContiguousBatch`.

3) Change all ingest signatures:
- `SessionStore::ingest_remote_batch(&mut self, batch: ContiguousBatch, now_ms: u64)`
- `ReplIngestRequest` should carry `ContiguousBatch`
- Callers no longer pass raw Vecs.

4) Provide `events()` / `into_events()` accessors for local processing, but do not expose mutation of the underlying Vec.

This makes it impossible to call ingest with an invalid batch; the compiler becomes the guardrail.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The only way to call `ingest_remote_batch` is via a `ContiguousBatch` (no raw Vec API).
- [ ] `ContiguousBatch::try_new` rejects mixed origin/namespace, gaps, duplicates, and prev_sha mismatches.
- [ ] `GapBuffer` and any other repl sources construct `ContiguousBatch` at the boundary.
- [ ] Tests cover: valid contiguous batch, gap rejection, duplicate rejection, mixed origin rejection.
- [ ] No code path can compile if it tries to ingest a raw Vec.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local

Disable checkpoints in tests by default

darin@book
title: darin@book
Use BranchName for all branch fields
description:
**Problem**
We already have a validated `BranchName` type (`identity.rs`) but branch fields still use raw `Option<String>` across core and wire. This means invalid branch names (empty/whitespace, spaces/newlines) can enter state or be emitted on the wire even though the type exists to prevent it. It weakens invariants in creation/closure metadata and spreads stringly-typed branch logic across the stack.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-core/src/identity.rs:832` — `BranchName` type + validation.
- `crates/beads-core/src/bead.rs:22` — `Creation.on_branch: Option<String>`.
- `crates/beads-core/src/composite.rs:65` — `Closure.on_branch: Option<String>`.
- `crates/beads-core/src/wire_bead.rs:309` / `:343` — `created_on_branch` / `closed_on_branch` are `Option<String>`.
- `crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs:45` — wire paths pass raw strings.

**Design**
Use `BranchName` everywhere branch names appear and let serde handle string conversion.

- Replace `Option<String>` with `Option<BranchName>` in:
  - `Creation` / `BeadCore` (`created_on_branch`)
  - `Closure` (`on_branch`)
  - `WireBeadFull` / `WireBeadPatch` (`created_on_branch`, `closed_on_branch`)
  - Git wire structs that carry branch names
- Use existing `#[serde(try_from = "String", into = "String")]` on `BranchName` to keep wire format unchanged while enforcing validation at decode time.
- Update encode/decode in `event.rs` / `wire_bead.rs` to call `BranchName::parse` and surface errors early.
- Update CLI parsing to produce `BranchName` (keep error messages helpful).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No `created_on_branch` / `closed_on_branch` fields remain as raw `String` in core/wire types.
- [ ] Decoding invalid branch names fails with actionable errors.
- [ ] Existing serialization format remains string-compatible; roundtrip tests pass.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-core/src/identity.rs`
- `crates/beads-core/src/bead.rs`
- `crates/beads-core/src/composite.rs`
- `crates/beads-core/src/wire_bead.rs`
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs`

title: Core state module naming cleanup
description:
**Problem**
- `src/core/state.rs`, `src/core/store_state.rs`, and `src/core/stores.rs` are easy to confuse.
- `stores.rs` contains dep/tombstone stores, not store state.

**Files:**
- src/core/state.rs
- src/core/store_state.rs
- src/core/stores.rs
- src/core/mod.rs
- imports throughout src/
design:
- Rename `stores.rs` to `dep_tombstone_store.rs` (or similar).
- Rename `store_state.rs` to `namespaced_state.rs` (or similar) while keeping `StoreState` type.
- Update module exports and imports; add re-export aliases if needed to avoid public break.
acceptance_criteria:
