- [ ] `core/mod.rs` exports remain stable for external users.
- [ ] All code compiles and tests pass.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Stateright crash/timer/storage modeling for idempotency + durability
description:
**Problem**
Idempotency/durability models are hand-rolled without Stateright’s timer, crash/recover, or storage features. This misses realistic failure windows and makes drift likely.

**Goal**
Model idempotency + durability using ActorModel timers, storage, and crash/recover actions with production-backed logic.
design:
**Design**
1) Define an ActorModel where server actors use production functions for:
   - idempotency map updates (WalIndex client_request_id mapping)
   - durability proof/receipt generation (`DurabilityReceipt`, `DurabilityCoordinator`)
2) Use Stateright timers (`model_timeout`) for durability wait + retry timeouts.
3) Enable crash/recover actions with persistent `Storage` to model WAL/index persistence.
4) Properties:
   - no remint of txn_id/event_ids on retry
   - timeout receipts are retryable and monotonic
   - crash after fsync but before reply still yields original receipt on retry

**References**
- `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/examples/timers.rs`
- `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/src/actor/model.rs` (Crash/Recover actions)
- Production idempotency: `src/daemon/wal/index.rs`
- Durability: `src/daemon/durability_coordinator.rs`, `src/core/durability.rs`
acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] ActorModel for idempotency/durability uses timers + crash/recover + storage.
- [ ] Properties cover retry stability + timeout semantics.
- [ ] Model uses production logic paths (no toy idempotency mapping).

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Repl Events must use a canonical/verified frame type
description:
**Problem**
`ReplMessage::Events` carries raw `EventFrameV1` values. The type does not encode that the bytes are canonical or that `sha256` matches the bytes. Today call sites *happen* to build frames from canonical sources, but nothing stops a future caller (or test harness) from constructing non-canonical frames and sending them. The compiler cannot help, and correctness depends on developer discipline.

This is a lie in the type system: `EventFrameV1` looks like a verified payload but is not. It violates parse-don’t-validate and creates scatter (every call site must remember to validate).

**Impact**
- Wire protocol can emit invalid frames (non-canonical bytes or wrong sha).
- Downstream peers reject or diverge; hard to localize to the sender.
- Future refactors can silently introduce invalid frames.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs` (EventFrameV1)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/manager.rs` / `server.rs` / `want.rs` (frame construction + send)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/broadcast.rs` (BroadcastEvent bytes type)
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Make the type tell the truth: only canonical frames can be sent.

Option A (preferred): parameterize or wrap frames by byte canonicality.
- Introduce `CanonicalFrame` (or `VerifiedFrame`) that holds `EventBytes<Canonical>` and derived `sha256`.
- Provide `impl From<VerifiedEvent<PrevVerified>> for CanonicalFrame` and `impl From<BroadcastEvent>` only if `BroadcastEvent` is also canonical.
- Change `ReplMessage::Events` to hold `Vec<CanonicalFrame>` (or `Vec<EventFrameV1<Canonical>>` if you add a type parameter).
- `encode_event_frame` accepts only canonical frames; `decode_event_frame` still yields raw frames which must be validated via `verify_event_frame` to become canonical.

Option B (minimal): keep `EventFrameV1` wire type but add a strict newtype `SendableFrame` used by all send paths; `SendableFrame::try_from(EventFrameV1)` validates canonical bytes + sha match and is the only type accepted by send functions.

In both options, the compiler forces validation before send.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] It is impossible to call repl send paths with a non‑canonical frame at compile time.
- [ ] The only conversion into a sendable frame performs canonical/sha validation.
- [ ] `ReplMessage::Events` and send helpers use the verified type (not raw `EventFrameV1`).
- [ ] Tests cover: non-canonical bytes rejected; sha mismatch rejected; canonical frame accepted.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
title: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
Migration: make imported comment NoteIds globally unique
description:
## What's Wrong
Go comment notes are imported with `NoteId::new(c.id.to_string())`. If comment IDs are not globally unique across issues in beads-go exports, notes can collide and overwrite/merge unexpectedly.

## Where
- src/migrate/go_export.rs:288 (NoteId::new(c.id.to_string()))

## Why It Matters
Data loss / silent merging during migration: two different comments could end up with the same note ID.

## Suggested Fix
- Make note IDs include the issue ID (and/or created_at) e.g. `go-comment-<issue-id>-<comment-id>`.
- Add a migration test fixture with repeated comment IDs across different issues to verify uniqueness.

## Acceptance
- Import never drops/merges distinct comments due to note ID collisions.
closed_reason: Made imported comment NoteIds globally unique by including issue ID in the format: go-comment-{issue_id}-{comment_id}. Added tests to verify uniqueness across issues.

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Realtime v0.5 implementation
description: Implement REALTIME_SPEC_DRAFT v0.5 per REALTIME_PLAN.md. Track phases 1-7 (REALTIME_PLAN.md §19) and their dependencies.
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 1: Core identity types + StoreMeta
description:
**Problem**
The realtime lane needs a canonical identity basis; core currently only models ActorId/BeadId/NoteId and still keys correctness by RemoteUrl. Without StoreId/ReplicaId/TxnId/ClientRequestId/StoreEpoch/SegmentId and StoreIdentity, we cannot define stable event identity, receipts, or WAL indexing.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.1 (StoreId as correctness identity), 2.1 (identity types), 15.1 (store meta file)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (section 1 Identity types, StoreIdentity)

**Files:** src/core/identity.rs, src/core/store_meta.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/lib.rs, Cargo.toml
design:
**Design**
- Add newtypes: StoreId, StoreEpoch, ReplicaId, TxnId, ClientRequestId, SegmentId using uuid::Uuid and serde.
- Add StoreIdentity { store_id, store_epoch } as a separate type so the store pair is not duplicated or passed as raw fields.
- Add StoreMeta in src/core/store_meta.rs with fields from the plan: store_id, store_epoch, replica_id, store_format_version, wal_format_version, checkpoint_format_version, replication_protocol_version, index_schema_version, created_at_ms.
- Expose constructors and parsing helpers, plus serde roundtrip tests for each newtype and StoreMeta.
- Re-export new types via src/core/mod.rs and src/lib.rs, and keep ActorId/BeadId/NoteId unchanged.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New identity types compile and serialize/deserialize (unit tests).
- [ ] StoreMeta matches plan fields and roundtrips via serde.
- [ ] StoreIdentity is used where a pair is required (no duplicated store_id/store_epoch fields).
- [ ] cargo check passes.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 3: SQLite WAL index + WalIndex traits
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: add WAL SQLite index with schema and reader and writer traits. There is no SQLite index today; WAL is a snapshot file. The realtime plan requires a rebuildable index for event lookup, idempotency, and watermarks.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11 (schema decisions) and §6.2 (table layout)
- SQLite PRAGMA choices in §6.4
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, Cargo.toml (rusqlite)
design:
**Design**
- Implement schema for events, watermarks, client_requests, origin_seq, segments, meta (per plan).
- Add WalIndexWriter and WalIndexReader traits with methods from REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.1.
- Use UUIDs as 16 byte BLOBs. Store event_ids as CBOR encoded Vec<EventId> in client_requests.
- Configure SQLite with PRAGMA: journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL (configurable), foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout.
- Add schema version in meta table and enforce upgrade rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Database initializes with expected tables and indices.
- [ ] WalIndexWriter and WalIndexReader trait methods are implemented.
- [ ] Schema version is stored and validated on open.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
admin.flush missing (fsync + optional checkpoint trigger)
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §16.2 specifies `admin.flush` to force fsync for a namespace (and optionally trigger a checkpoint). There is no admin flush handler or IPC/CLI surface; `src/daemon/admin.rs` only has status/metrics/doctor/scrub/reload/maintenance/rotate. This leaves no explicit manual durability/flush hook.

**Design**
- Add admin IPC op + CLI subcommand `bd admin flush [--namespace] [--checkpoint-now]` (namespace default core).
- Implement daemon handler to call WAL fsync for the namespace and optionally enqueue checkpoint for affected groups.
- Surface in `src/api` + renderers; include timing/durability info in response.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] IPC + CLI `admin flush` exists and returns success payload.
- [ ] WAL fsync performed for requested namespace.
- [ ] `--checkpoint-now` schedules a checkpoint for groups including that namespace.
- [ ] Tests cover handler + response shape.

**Files:** src/daemon/admin.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/render.rs, src/api
darin@book
title: darin@book
enforce 0600/0700 perms for store policy files + WAL dirs
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §13.4 requires sensitive store-local files to be 0600 (namespaces.toml, replicas.toml, wal.sqlite, meta.json) and WAL namespace dirs to be 0700 with symlink escape prevention. Current code enforces 0600 for store_meta and wal.sqlite, but `load_namespace_policies` + `load_replica_roster` read files without permission checks, and WAL namespace dirs are created with `create_dir_all` in `src/daemon/wal/segment.rs` without symlink checks or explicit 0700 perms.

**Design**
- Add a shared helper to enforce secure perms + reject symlinks for store-local config files.
- Apply to `namespaces.toml` and `replicas.toml` read/write paths.
- When creating `wal/<namespace>/` directories, set 0700 and reject symlink components (similar to store lock).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] namespaces.toml + replicas.toml are 0600 (or corrected on load) and symlinked paths are rejected.
- [ ] wal/<namespace>/ directories are 0700 and symlink escapes are rejected.
- [ ] Tests cover permission enforcement and symlink rejection.

**Files:** src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/wal/segment.rs, src/paths.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
Checkpoint export always full; dirty shard tracking + previous reuse missing
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §13.2/§13.4 call for incremental checkpoint export: track dirty shards per namespace and reuse previous checkpoint blobs for unchanged shards. Current `build_snapshot` in `src/git/checkpoint/export.rs` sets `dirty_shards = all shards`, and `GitWorker::publish_checkpoint` always calls `export_checkpoint(previous=None)`, so every checkpoint is a full re‑export with no reuse.

**Design**
- Track dirty shard IDs in `StoreRuntime` using `ApplyOutcome` (beads/tombstones/deps) and pass them into `CheckpointSnapshot` instead of defaulting to all.
- Retain the last export per group (or load from cache) and pass `previous: Some(&export)` to `export_checkpoint` so unchanged shard files are reused.
- Ensure dirty shards are cleared only after successful publish.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Dirty shards are tracked per namespace and carried into checkpoint snapshots.
- [ ] `export_checkpoint` reuses previous files for clean shards.
- [ ] Checkpoint export tests cover dirty‑only updates and reuse behavior.

**Files:** src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/checkpoint_scheduler.rs, src/daemon/git_worker.rs, src/git/checkpoint/export.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
Replication EVENTS batching is not round‑robin across (namespace, origin)
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §9.3 requires EVENTS batches be constructed round‑robin across eligible (namespace, origin_replica_id) to avoid starvation. Current `handle_want` in `src/daemon/repl/manager.rs` and `server.rs` builds batches by iterating hot‑cache then WAL ranges in map order; there is no round‑robin scheduling.

**Design**
- Implement a round‑robin iterator over keys `(namespace, origin)` when building EVENTS frames.
- Preserve per‑origin contiguity (do not interleave out of order within a key).
- Add tests that show fairness across multiple namespaces/origins under batch size limits.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] EVENTS batching uses round‑robin across (namespace, origin) when filling a batch.
- [ ] Per‑origin sequence ordering is preserved.
- [ ] Tests validate fairness and contiguity.

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/manager.rs, src/daemon/repl/server.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
WalIndex durability mode is hardcoded to cache
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §6.1/§6.2 calls for a store‑local `index_durability_mode = cache | durable` setting. The code always opens `SqliteWalIndex` with `IndexDurabilityMode::Cache` (e.g., `StoreRuntime::open`, fsck/rebuild helpers). There is no config knob or store‑local setting.

**Design**
- Add a store‑local setting (namespaces.toml or a new `store_config.toml`) or config‑layer override to choose `cache` vs `durable`.
- Thread the setting into `SqliteWalIndex::open` calls, including rebuild/fsck paths.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Index durability mode is configurable and persisted per store.
- [ ] All index open paths respect the configured mode.
- [ ] Tests cover both modes.

**Files:** src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/daemon/wal/fsck.rs, src/config.rs (or new config module)
darin@book
title: darin@book
wal.sqlite checkpoint/truncate not scheduled
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §6.2 notes we should periodically run `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` for `wal.sqlite` to control the SQLite WAL file. There is no code path invoking `wal_checkpoint` at all.

**Design**
- Add a background/maintenance hook (e.g., in checkpoint scheduler loop or on a timer) to run `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` on wal.sqlite.
- Make frequency configurable or piggy‑back on existing periodic tasks.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) runs periodically.
- [ ] Frequency is configurable or documented.
- [ ] Tests or instrumentation confirm it triggers.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/daemon/core.rs (or scheduler)
darin@book
title: darin@book
Checkpoint meta never includes roster_hash
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §13.3 calls for `roster_hash` in checkpoint meta and warnings on mismatch. `StoreRuntime::checkpoint_snapshot` always sets `roster_hash = None`, and there is no roster hash computation anywhere. This loses the audit trail for durability eligibility changes.

**Design**
- Compute roster hash from validated `replicas.toml` (canonical JSON) when present.
- Include it in `CheckpointSnapshot` + meta; surface mismatch warnings on import.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] roster_hash is present when a replica roster exists.
- [ ] Checkpoint import compares roster_hash and emits a warning on mismatch.
- [ ] Tests cover hash stability + mismatch warning.

**Files:** src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/git/checkpoint/meta.rs, src/git/checkpoint/import.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
Phase 3: WAL replay + index rebuild
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: implement WAL replay and SQLite index rebuild from WAL segments, including tail truncation rules. Without this, crash recovery, idempotency, and origin_seq allocation are unsafe.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.3 (index rebuild and catch up), §6.5 (crash consistency), §7.3 step 8 (replay)
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/replay.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement scan of wal/<namespace>/segment-*.wal in creation order.
- Validate segment headers (store_id, store_epoch, namespace, wal_format_version) before replay.
- For each segment, read frames from last_indexed_offset (if present) to EOF and index records.
- On tail corruption, truncate the segment to the last valid frame boundary and update segments table.
- Provide full rebuild path when index is missing or schema mismatch.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rebuild populates events, watermarks, and origin_seq tables from WAL segments.
- [ ] Tail truncation is handled safely during replay.
- [ ] Startup can catch up from last_indexed_offset without full scan.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: MutationEngine planning + request hash
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: implement a MutationEngine that turns IPC ops into a single EventBody with canonical request hash and stamps. Current mutations in `src/daemon/executor.rs` mutate state directly; there is no event planning or idempotency digest.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §8.1 and §8.2 (mutation planning and request_sha256)
- request_sha256 storage in WAL headers (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11)
**Files:** src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs (new), src/daemon/ops.rs, src/daemon/clock.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a MutationEngine that validates inputs, enforces limits (MAX_OPS_PER_TXN, MAX_NOTE_BYTES), and builds one EventBody (TxnV1).
- Compute canonical request_sha256 from a normalized request that excludes retry only fields (wait_timeout_ms, durability wait targets).
- Mint txn_id and stamps using HLC; ensure event_time_ms equals stamp wall time and populate hlc_max.
- Return EventDraft { event_body, request_sha256, client_request_id } for WAL append.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] MutationEngine builds a deterministic EventBody draft plus request_sha256 from a canonicalized request.
- [ ] Limit enforcement rejects oversize requests before WAL append.
- [ ] MutationEngine produces plan output without mutating CanonicalState.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: Daemon events first mutation pipeline
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: integrate the events first mutation pipeline in the daemon. Current `src/daemon/executor.rs` mutates CanonicalState directly and writes snapshot WAL. We need WAL append plus deterministic apply_event and watermark updates.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §8.3 (apply pipeline) and §6.5 (crash consistency ordering)
- StoreRuntime integration in §7.1
**Files:** src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, src/daemon/query.rs
design:
**Design**
- Replace direct mutation calls with: MutationEngine -> EventWal append -> core::apply_event -> update indexes and watermarks -> schedule replication and checkpoint.
- Enforce idempotency via WAL index lookup before creating a new event when client_request_id is present.
- Persist HLC state and idempotency mapping in the same WAL append critical section.
- Ensure responses are returned only after apply and watermark updates complete.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All mutation handlers use the events-first pipeline (plan -> WAL append -> apply_event).
- [ ] Idempotent retry returns prior txn_id/event_ids without writing a new event.
- [ ] Watermarks advance only after apply_event completes.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: IPC requests + receipts (namespace, durability, client_request_id)
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: update IPC response shapes for receipts and error payloads. Current `src/daemon/ipc.rs` does not return DurabilityReceipt, and ErrorPayload does not match REALTIME_ERRORS.md.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (IPC protocol evolution), §16.1 (response fields and error payload), §16.3 (CLI flags)

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/api/mod.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/*, REALTIME_ERRORS.md

**Design**
- Add DurabilityReceipt to success responses and to timeout errors when waiting for replicated durability.
- Replace ErrorPayload shape to `{ code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? }` and keep error codes aligned with REALTIME_ERRORS.md.
- Ensure API JSON types expose receipts without lossy view structs.
- Request field additions and CLI flag plumbing live in bd-3m5.51.
design:
**Design**
- Add optional `namespace`, `durability`, and `client_request_id` fields to mutation requests with defaults (namespace=core, durability=LocalFsync).
- Add DurabilityReceipt to success responses and to timeout errors when waiting for replicated durability.
- Replace ErrorPayload shape to `{ code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? }` and keep error codes aligned with REALTIME_ERRORS.md.
- Plumb CLI flags `--namespace`, `--durability`, `--client-request-id` to IPC requests.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] IPC mutation success responses include DurabilityReceipt in JSON mode.
- [ ] Durability timeouts return ErrorPayload with retryable=true and receipt populated.
- [ ] ErrorPayload schema matches REALTIME_ERRORS.md (code/message/retryable/details/receipt).
- [ ] API JSON types expose receipts without lossy view structs.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: Replication protocol types + framing
description:
**Problem**
Replication needs a typed, verifiable on wire protocol. Today there are no CBOR frame types or verification state; without a typestate pipeline, callers can accidentally skip hash or prev link checks and still treat events as valid.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.1-9.6 (frame definitions and rules), §0.6 (hashing over EventBody bytes)
- REALTIME_ERRORS.md (error payload shape)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (EventBytes, EventFrameV1, VerifiedEventAny)

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/frame.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/proto.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/mod.rs (new), src/core/event.rs
design:
**Design**
- Define CBOR envelope `{ v, type, body }` and per-message bodies (HELLO, WELCOME, EVENTS, ACK, WANT, ERROR) with spec field names.
- Introduce EventFrameV1 that carries EventId, sha256, prev_sha256, and EventBytes<Opaque>.
- Add verification pipeline types: VerifiedEvent<PrevVerified> vs VerifiedEvent<PrevDeferred> (or equivalent) so callers cannot treat a deferred prev check as fully verified.
- Enforce MAX_FRAME_BYTES, MAX_EVENT_BATCH_BYTES, and decoding limits before allocating large buffers; reject oversize frames with ERROR(code="frame_too_large").
- Keep ErrorPayload consistent with IPC; ERROR frames use the same schema and codes.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Encode/decode roundtrip tests for each replication message type.
- [ ] VerifiedEvent typestate prevents forwarding events with deferred prev checks.
- [ ] Oversized frames and batches are rejected deterministically with correct error codes.
- [ ] Error frames serialize to the same schema as IPC ErrorPayload.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: EventBroadcaster + hot cache
description:
**Problem**
Phase 5: add a bounded EventBroadcaster and hot cache for recently appended events. There is no pub or sub mechanism to stream events or serve fast replication reads.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §7.5 (EventBroadcaster and hot cache requirements)
**Files:** src/daemon/broadcast.rs (new) or src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement a broadcaster that publishes EventId, sha, prev_sha, namespace, and canonical EventBody bytes.
- Enforce bounds by events and bytes; drop slow subscribers with ERROR(code="subscriber_lagged").
- Maintain a FIFO hot cache with deterministic eviction.
- Integrate publish after WAL append in StoreRuntime.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Broadcaster delivers events in order to subscribers and drops slow consumers when bounds exceeded.
- [ ] Hot cache evicts by size and event count deterministically.
- [ ] API exposes publish/subscribe with bounded channels and a drop reason.
- [ ] Unit tests cover backpressure and eviction behavior.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: Replication runtime integration
description:
**Problem**
This bead is now the integration glue for replication runtime. Sessions/manager/server are split into separate beads; we need to wire them into the daemon coordinator with bounded channels, correct defaults, and backpressure so replication traffic is serialized safely.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md sections 7.5, 9.1-9.8, 7.2 (store runtime wiring)
- Split beads: bd-3m5.46 (session), bd-3m5.47 (manager), bd-3m5.48 (server)
**Files:** src/daemon/repl/mod.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/run.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a repl module root to share wiring/config between session/manager/server.
- On daemon startup, spawn replication server + manager threads and register bounded channels into the coordinator.
- Route inbound replication ingest through the coordinator (WAL append + apply) and produce ACKs via session channel.
- Route outbound fanout from EventBroadcaster (with WAL fallback) and enforce AdmissionController limits at ingress.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Daemon startup wires replication server + manager and registers channels to the coordinator.
- [ ] Inbound replication events reach the coordinator through bounded queues and produce ACKs.
- [ ] Outbound sessions receive events via broadcaster or WAL range fallback.
- [ ] Overload path rejects replication ingest (no unbounded buffering).

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: DurabilityCoordinator + peer ACK tracking
description:
**Problem**
Phase 5: implement DurabilityCoordinator for LocalFsync and ReplicatedFsync(k). There is no coordinator to track peer ACKs or advance durable watermarks beyond local fsync.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §10 (durability coordination), §0.12 (applied vs durable watermarks), §2.1.2 (replica roster)
**Files:** src/daemon/durability_coordinator.rs (new), src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/repl/*
design:
**Design**
- Track pending txns by txn_id and required durability class.
- For ReplicatedFsync(k), select eligible replicas (from roster if present) and wait for durable ACKs.
- On success, return updated receipt with durability_proof and advance durable watermarks.
- On insufficient eligible replicas, return ERROR(code="durability_unavailable").
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ReplicatedFsync(k) completes when k eligible replicas ACK.
- [ ] Insufficient eligible replicas triggers immediate error.
- [ ] Durable watermark advances only after local fsync and required ACKs.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 6: Checkpoint canonical JSON + manifest/meta structs
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: implement canonical JSON helper and checkpoint manifest and meta structs. There is no checkpoint lane module yet, so manifest and meta definitions do not exist.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.3-13.5 (canonical JSON, manifest.json, meta.json)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/json_canon.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/manifest.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/meta.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/mod.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Add `to_canon_json_bytes<T: Serialize>(v: &T) -> Vec<u8>` with sorted key ordering and no extra whitespace.
- Define Manifest and Meta structs with fields from plan; implement content_hash and manifest_hash calculation.
- Add tests verifying canonical JSON stability and hash determinism.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canonical JSON helper emits stable bytes across runs.
- [ ] manifest_hash and content_hash computed per plan.
- [ ] Tests cover a minimal checkpoint meta example.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 1: Namespaces + StoreState
description:
**Problem**
The core state is currently single namespace. Namespace boundaries are implicit ("core") and there is no NamespaceId or namespace policy type, which makes cross namespace bugs easy and prevents typestate around per namespace invariants.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.3 (StoreState choice), 0.4 (namespace policy representation), 2.2 (Namespaces)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (section 3 Namespaces)

**Files:** src/core/namespace.rs (new), src/core/store_state.rs (new) or src/core/state.rs, src/core/mod.rs, src/lib.rs, src/config.rs (policy parsing hook)
design:
**Design**
- Add NamespaceId newtype with validation [a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,31}, matching the stateright model; include as_str and display.
- Add NamespacePolicy and CheckpointGroup structs with the fields and defaults from REALTIME_PLAN.md 2.2.
- Introduce StoreState as BTreeMap<NamespaceId, CanonicalState> with helpers: get, get_mut, ensure_namespace, and a constant default NamespaceId::core().
- Ensure callers must select a namespace before accessing a CanonicalState (no implicit global operations), so cross namespace operations require explicit opt in.
- Re-export NamespaceId, NamespacePolicy, CheckpointGroup, StoreState in src/core/mod.rs and src/lib.rs.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] NamespaceId validation tests cover valid and invalid inputs.
- [ ] StoreState can create and return the default core namespace without side effects.
- [ ] NamespacePolicy defaults match the plan.
- [ ] No daemon behavior change yet; this is core types only.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 6: Checkpoint export + cache
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: implement checkpoint export to sharded JSONL plus local cache. There is no exporter for the new checkpoint layout.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.1, §13.3, §13.8 (layout, sharding, export, cache)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/export.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/layout.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/cache.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/mod.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement sharded JSONL layout under namespaces/<ns>/{state,tombstones,deps}/00..ff.jsonl.
- Serialize lines in canonical JSON with deterministic sorting by key.
- Write manifest.json and meta.json with hashes and included watermarks.
- Add local checkpoint cache under store_dir/checkpoint_cache/<group>/ with atomic publish (tmp + rename + fsync).
- Add tests verifying shard ordering and manifest hashes for a small StoreState.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Export writes shard files, manifest.json, and meta.json with correct hashes.
- [ ] Cache publish is atomic and uses temp dirs.
- [ ] Export output is deterministic for a given StoreState (same bytes).

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 6: Checkpoint import + verification
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: implement checkpoint import and verification (manifest_hash and content_hash). There is no importer for the new checkpoint layout.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.9 and §13.9.1 (import, verification, JSONL bounds)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/import.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/layout.rs, src/git/checkpoint/mod.rs
design:
**Design**
- Read checkpoint tree or cache, verify manifest hashes and file hashes.
- Validate content_hash from meta preimage.
- Parse JSONL shards line by line with size bounds and merge into StoreState deterministically.
- Return included watermarks and optional included_heads for seeding head sha.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Import rejects mismatched hashes.
- [ ] JSONL parsing enforces line and shard size limits.
- [ ] Merge output is deterministic for identical inputs.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 6: Checkpoint scheduler + git ref wiring
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: integrate checkpoint scheduling and git ref wiring. Export and import modules alone are not used until daemon schedules checkpoints and writes the new refs.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.2 (refs), §14 (scheduler), §7.3 (startup flow)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/*, src/daemon/scheduler.rs or new checkpoint runtime module, src/daemon/git_worker.rs, src/git/sync.rs (legacy)
design:
**Design**
- Add checkpoint runtime that tracks dirty namespaces and schedules exports with debounce, max interval, and max events.
- Write checkpoint commits to `refs/beads/<store_id>/<group>` and keep `refs/beads/meta` for discovery.
- Ensure only one push in flight per group and coalesce when new events arrive.
- Keep legacy `src/git/sync.rs` as read only migration path.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Checkpoint export is scheduled and produces commits under new refs.
- [ ] Dirty events during export schedule a follow up checkpoint.
- [ ] Legacy sync remains available for migration but does not write new realtime refs.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 7: IPC Subscribe streaming + require_min_seen
description:
**Problem**
Phase 7: implement IPC Subscribe streaming with require_min_seen gating. IPC is currently request or response only and has no streaming path.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §11 (local subscriptions), §16.1 (require_min_seen and timeout semantics)
**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/daemon/server.rs, src/daemon/broadcast.rs, src/api/mod.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a Subscribe request variant with namespace, require_min_seen, and wait_timeout_ms.
- Stream EventBody bytes plus sha and prev_sha headers.
- Use EventBroadcaster for live updates and WAL read for historical backfill.
- If require_min_seen is not satisfied, wait up to wait_timeout_ms then return retryable error with current watermarks.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Subscribe streams events in order and closes on disconnect.
- [ ] require_min_seen gating works and returns retryable errors on timeout.
- [ ] Backpressure limits prevent unbounded buffering.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 7: CLI subscribe command
description:
**Problem**
Phase 7: add CLI support for streaming subscriptions. Without a CLI command, subscribe is hard to validate and use.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.3 (CLI changes)
**Files:** src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/*, src/cli/render.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a `bd subscribe` command with flags for namespace and require_min_seen.
- Stream events to stdout in json and human modes (consistent with other commands).
- Propagate retryable errors and exit with non zero status on permanent errors.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI subscribe connects and prints events in order.
- [ ] CLI handles retryable timeout errors and exits cleanly.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes for CLI tests if present.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 1: Core error types + ErrorPayload
description:
**Problem**
We need a single, truthful error vocabulary shared by IPC and replication. Today error mapping is scattered (OpError/CoreError/IpcError) and ErrorPayload in src/daemon/ipc.rs lacks retryable/receipt fields. That creates multiple representations of the same failure and forces clients to interpret strings ad hoc.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (error payload standardization) and §9.10 (replication error handling)
- REALTIME_ERRORS.md (canonical error code registry)
- Current error plumbing: src/core/error.rs, src/error.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/daemon/repl/proto.rs (new)

**Files:** src/core/error.rs (new or expanded), src/error.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/api/mod.rs, src/daemon/repl/proto.rs, REALTIME_ERRORS.md
design:
**Design**
- Introduce ErrorCode enum with stable string mappings for every code in REALTIME_ERRORS.md; make it the single source of truth for code strings.
- Define ErrorPayload { code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? } in core and re-use it in IPC JSON and replication CBOR.
- Provide From conversions from CoreError/OpError/IpcError/SyncError into ErrorPayload with consistent retryable/effect semantics.
- Enforce that unknown codes are tolerated by clients; only retry when retryable=true.
- Keep ErrorPayload schema version stable; add fields only as optional to preserve compatibility.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ErrorCode covers every entry in REALTIME_ERRORS.md and is the sole mapping to code strings.
- [ ] ErrorPayload serializes to both JSON (IPC) and CBOR (replication) with optional fields omitted when empty.
- [ ] Tests verify retryable and receipt fields are preserved and unknown codes do not break decoding.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: HLC persistence + recovery
description:
**Problem**
HLC state must be persisted to prevent stamp regression after restart or wall-clock rollback. Recovery must reconstruct HLC from WAL if SQLite is missing.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §2.8 (write stamps explicit requirements)
- §6.2 (hlc table in SQLite schema)

**Design**
- Add `hlc` table to SQLite schema: (actor_id TEXT PK, last_physical_ms INTEGER, last_logical INTEGER)
- On startup: initialize HLC from max(persisted, system_time)
- On mint: clamp forward jumps to HLC_MAX_FORWARD_DRIFT_MS (default 600s), log anomaly
- On index rebuild: scan WAL records for hlc_max and take max per ActorId
- Persist HLC in same critical section as WAL append/index commit

**Acceptance**
- [ ] HLC state persisted in SQLite hlc table
- [ ] Restart recovers HLC without stamp regression
- [ ] Forward jump clamping logged as anomaly
- [ ] Index rebuild reconstructs HLC from WAL hlc_max

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/core/time.rs, src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 3: WatermarkHeadMap + head sha tracking
description:
**Problem**
Head sha tracking is required to validate prev_sha continuity and to safely advance watermarks. Today we only have seq values, which allows the system to represent seq>0 without knowing the chain head, making continuity checks ambiguous. This bead scopes the Phase 3 core tracking (no replication session wiring).

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.12 (head sha rule) + 2.3 (WatermarkHeadMap)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (HeadStatus)

**Design**
- Extend watermarks to carry head hash for each (namespace, origin) pair (HeadStatus: Genesis, Known, Unknown).
- Require head to be Known whenever seq>0; Unknown is allowed only when a checkpoint does not include heads.
- Persist head sha in the wal.sqlite watermarks table (applied_head_sha, durable_head_sha) and keep in memory in StoreRuntime.
- Update head sha on WAL append (durable) and after apply_event (applied) so prev_sha validation can use the latest known head.
- Provide helper API: advance_contiguous(seq, head) and observe_at_least(seq, head) to enforce invariants in one place.
- Replication session wiring deferred to Phase 5 (bd-3m5.46).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Head sha is persisted and reloaded alongside applied and durable watermarks.
- [ ] API rejects advancing a nonzero seq without a head sha.
- [ ] StoreRuntime exposes head sha lookup per (namespace, origin) for later prev_sha validation.
- [ ] Head sha updates are tied to watermark advancement (no stale head).

**Files:** src/core/watermark.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/wal/index.rs
design:
**Design**
- Extend watermarks to carry head hash for each (namespace, origin) pair (HeadStatus: Genesis, Known, Unknown).
- Require head to be Known whenever seq>0; Unknown is allowed only when a checkpoint does not include heads.
- Persist head sha in the wal.sqlite watermarks table (applied_head_sha, durable_head_sha) and keep in memory in StoreRuntime.
- Update head sha on WAL append (durable) and after apply_event (applied) so prev_sha validation can use the latest known head.
- Provide helper API: advance_contiguous(seq, head) and observe_at_least(seq, head) to enforce invariants in one place.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Head sha is persisted and reloaded alongside applied and durable watermarks.
- [ ] API rejects advancing a nonzero seq without a head sha.
- [ ] StoreRuntime exposes head sha lookup per (namespace, origin) for later prev_sha validation.
- [ ] Head sha updates are tied to watermark advancement (no stale head).

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: GapBufferByNsOrigin for replication
description:
**Problem**
Out-of-order replication needs a bounded gap buffer that is aware of contiguity and prev hash verification. A naive BTreeMap of bytes lets callers accidentally treat deferred prev checks as safe and doesn’t encode the decision outcomes.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.4 (contiguous ingest rule), §9.8 (gap handling + bounds)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (GapBuffer, VerifiedEventAny, IngestDecision)

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/session.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement GapBufferByNsOrigin keyed by (NamespaceId, ReplicaId) with per-origin gap state.
- Store VerifiedEventAny so deferred prev checks remain explicit; only contiguous VerifiedEvent<PrevVerified> are forwarded to WAL append.
- Provide ingest API returning an IngestDecision: ForwardContiguousBatch, BufferedNeedWant, DuplicateNoop, Reject(code).
- Enforce per-origin bounds on buffered events and bytes, plus a timeout window; overflow or timeout returns Reject("gap_buffer_overflow"/"gap_timeout").
- Drain buffered suffix in order when the predecessor arrives; stop if a deferred event is still waiting on prev verification.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Gap buffer enforces event and byte bounds and returns explicit Reject on overflow/timeout.
- [ ] Only fully verified contiguous events are forwarded to WAL append.
- [ ] Duplicate events are treated as no-ops without disturbing the buffer.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: single gap, multiple gaps, overflow, timeout, deferred-prev behavior.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: AdmissionController + overload shedding
description:
**Problem**
Plan requires priority ordering and overload shedding to protect local mutations from replication storms.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19 (overload shedding, internal backpressure defaults)
- Priority: local IPC > replication ingest > WANT servicing > checkpoint

**Design**
- Add `AdmissionController` to src/daemon/admission.rs
- Track inflight counts: ipc_mutations, repl_ingest_bytes, repl_ingest_events
- Enforce limits: MAX_IPC_INFLIGHT_MUTATIONS (1024), MAX_REPL_INGEST_QUEUE_BYTES (32 MiB), MAX_REPL_INGEST_QUEUE_EVENTS (50k)
- When overloaded: shed replication first, then IPC
- Return ERROR(code="overloaded") with retry_after_ms

**Acceptance**
- [ ] AdmissionController tracks inflight by priority class
- [ ] Replication shed before IPC mutations
- [ ] Limits enforced with ERROR(code="overloaded")
- [ ] Tests verify shedding order

**Files:** src/daemon/admission.rs (new), src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/repl/session.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 1: Watermarks + durability types
description:
**Problem**
Realtime semantics require explicit applied vs durable watermarks and head hash tracking. Today there is no watermark type, no seq0 vs seq1 distinction, and receipts cannot represent pending or partial durability without ambiguity.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.12 (applied vs durable), 0.12 head sha rule, 10.1 (durability classes), 16.1 (receipt shape)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (Watermarks, Watermark, HeadStatus, DurabilityClass, DurabilityOutcome)

**Files:** src/core/watermark.rs (new), src/core/durability.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/lib.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add Seq0 and Seq1 newtypes: Seq0 for highest contiguous seen, Seq1 for actual event origin_seq (1 based). Provide helpers next(), prev(), prev_seq0().
- Add Watermark<K> that couples Seq0 with HeadStatus (Genesis, Known(sha), Unknown). This prevents representing seq>0 with no head.
- Add Watermarks<Applied> and Watermarks<Durable> maps keyed by (NamespaceId, ReplicaId) with helpers advance_contiguous and observe_at_least that enforce head requirements.
- Add DurabilityClass with ReplicatedFsync using NonZeroU32 for k; add DurabilityOutcome (Achieved vs Pending) and DurabilityProofV1 to express receipts without ambiguous Option fields.
- Keep types serde friendly for IPC and WAL metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] WatermarkMap and WatermarkHeadMap helpers enforce contiguous advance and head sha required for seq > 0.
- [ ] Seq0/Seq1 helpers behave as expected and are used by Watermark APIs.
- [ ] DurabilityClass cannot represent k=0; DurabilityOutcome encodes pending vs achieved explicitly.
- [ ] serde roundtrip tests for WatermarkMap/WatermarkHeadMap and DurabilityClass/Outcome.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 6: CheckpointSnapshot barrier type
description:
**Problem**
Checkpoint export requires a snapshot barrier that freezes the state and included watermarks together. Without a typed barrier, the coordinator can accidentally mix shards from different logical times or claim watermarks that aren’t represented in the exported content.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.8 (checkpoint snapshot barrier and consistency rule)
- Checkpoint scheduling in §13.7 and export workflow in §13.8

**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/types.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/export.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Introduce an immutable CheckpointSnapshot struct: { group_id, policy_hash, roster_hash, included_watermarks (durable), included_heads?, shard_payloads/dirty_shards, created_at_ms }.
- The coordinator thread captures the snapshot under a single lock so shard contents and watermarks correspond to the same logical point in time.
- Make the snapshot owned/immutable (e.g., Arc or owned values), so the worker thread cannot observe concurrent mutations.
- Enforce a check: included_watermarks must not exceed the max seq represented by shard_payloads; if it would, delay snapshot or recompute.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Snapshot captures included watermarks and shard contents from a single coordinator tick.
- [ ] Snapshot object is immutable once created and safe to pass to worker thread.
- [ ] Export worker consumes only snapshot data (no live reads during I/O).
- [ ] Snapshot includes metadata needed for manifest/meta construction without touching live state.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 7: admin.status + admin.metrics IPC ops
description:
**Problem**
Operators need introspection into store health, watermarks, segments, replication, and checkpoints.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (admin.status, admin.metrics)

**Design**
- admin.status returns: store_id, replica_id, namespaces, applied/durable watermarks, WAL segment stats (count/bytes), index health (last_indexed_offset per segment), replication sessions (peers, last_ack, lag), checkpoint groups (dirty/in-flight/last push)
- admin.metrics returns: counters (wal_append_ok/err, apply_ok/err, repl_events_in/out), gauges (IPC inflight, repl queue depth, per-peer lag), histograms (wal_append_duration, checkpoint_duration)
- Both return structured JSON

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.status returns complete store introspection
- [ ] admin.metrics returns counters/gauges/histograms
- [ ] CLI `bd admin status` and `bd admin metrics` work
- [ ] Tests verify expected fields

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/daemon/admin.rs (new), src/cli/commands/admin.rs (new)
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 7: admin.doctor + admin.scrub_now IPC ops
description:
**Problem**
Operators need bounded health checks with deterministic remediation actions.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (admin.doctor, admin.scrub_now)

**Design**
- admin.doctor: runs bounded health scrub, returns machine-readable JSON with:
  - checks: [{ id, status, severity, evidence, suggested_actions }]
  - summary: { risk, safe_to_accept_writes, safe_to_prune_wal, safe_to_rebuild_index }
  - Must include explicit remediation actions (exact admin ops to run)
- admin.scrub_now: samples N events per namespace (default 200), verifies WAL CRC32c and sha256, verifies SQLite offsets, optionally verifies checkpoint_cache
  - Returns structured pass/warn/fail results

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.doctor returns structured checks with remediation
- [ ] admin.scrub_now samples and verifies bounded records
- [ ] CLI `bd admin doctor` and `bd admin scrub` work
- [ ] Tests verify check detection

**Files:** src/daemon/admin.rs, src/daemon/scrubber.rs (new), src/cli/commands/admin.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 7: admin.maintenance_mode + admin.rebuild_index
description:
**Problem**
Operators need to put daemon in read-only mode for repairs and rebuild SQLite from WAL.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (admin.maintenance_mode, admin.rebuild_index)

**Design**
- admin.maintenance_mode: toggle read-only mode
  - When enabled: reject mutations/replication ingest with ERROR(code="maintenance_mode")
  - Allow admin/read operations
  - Required for online repair beyond tail truncation
- admin.rebuild_index: rebuilds SQLite index from WAL segments
  - Explicit operator action; requires maintenance_mode
  - Scans all WAL segments and re-indexes events

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.maintenance_mode toggles write rejection
- [ ] admin.rebuild_index scans WAL and rebuilds SQLite
- [ ] CLI `bd admin maintenance on/off` and `bd admin rebuild-index` work
- [ ] Tests verify mode transitions

**Files:** src/daemon/admin.rs, src/daemon/wal/replay.rs, src/cli/commands/admin.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 7: admin.fingerprint for divergence detection
description:
**Problem**
Operators need to quickly detect divergence across replicas using stable convergence fingerprints.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (admin.fingerprint, fingerprint algorithm)

**Design**
- Returns per-namespace: { state_sha256, tombstones_sha256, deps_sha256, namespace_root }
- Merkle-ish algorithm: shard_hash[00..ff] -> type_root -> namespace_root
- Modes: full (per-shard hashes, expensive), sample(N) (deterministic N samples)
- Sampling uses sha256(namespace || request_nonce) to select indices
- Includes watermarks_applied and watermarks_durable as basis

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.fingerprint returns namespace_root and type_roots
- [ ] full mode returns per-shard hashes
- [ ] sample(N) returns deterministic shard samples
- [ ] Tests verify determinism (same state -> same hash)

**Files:** src/daemon/admin.rs, src/daemon/fingerprint.rs (new), src/cli/commands/admin.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 7: admin.reload_policies + admin.rotate_replica_id
description:
**Problem**
Operators need to reload namespace policies live and recover from replica_id collision.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.4 (reload semantics), §2.1.1 (replica_id collision)

**Design**
- admin.reload_policies: reloads namespaces.toml, validates, applies safe live changes
  - Safe to apply: visibility, ready_eligible, retention windows, ttl_basis
  - Requires restart: checkpoint group membership, replication listen params
  - Returns diff summary (applied vs requires_restart)
- admin.rotate_replica_id: generates new replica_id, updates meta.json, logs old->new mapping
  - Used for recovery from duplicated store directories
  - New replica starts with origin_seq=0 for new identity

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.reload_policies applies safe changes, reports restart-required changes
- [ ] admin.rotate_replica_id generates new UUID and persists
- [ ] CLI `bd admin reload-policies` and `bd admin rotate-replica-id` work
- [ ] Tests verify policy reload diff

**Files:** src/daemon/admin.rs, src/core/store_meta.rs, src/cli/commands/admin.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 3: bd store unlock CLI for stale lock recovery
description:
**Problem**
Operators need to recover from stale lock files without manual rm.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.2 (store lock metadata, recovery ergonomics)

**Design**
- CLI: `bd store unlock --store-id <id>`
- Reads lock metadata and displays: store_id, replica_id, pid, started_at_ms, daemon_version
- If pid does not exist: removes lock file (safe case)
- If pid exists but different process (pid reuse): removes lock file
- If pid exists and appears to be daemon: requires --force flag
- Logs unlock action for traceability

**Acceptance**
- [ ] bd store unlock reads and displays lock metadata
- [ ] Auto-removes stale locks (pid gone)
- [ ] Requires --force for live-looking processes
- [ ] Tests verify safe/force paths

**Files:** src/cli/commands/store.rs (new), src/daemon/store_lock.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 3: bd store fsck offline verification tool
description:
**Problem**
Operators need offline tool to verify and repair WAL/index when daemon is stopped.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §5.2 (offline repair, online vs offline boundary)

**Design**
- CLI: `bd store fsck --store-id <id>`
- Scans wal/<ns> segments, validates headers, frame CRCs, sha256, prev_sha continuity, per-(ns,origin) contiguity
- Cross-checks wal.sqlite offsets if present
- Emits deterministic FsckReport schema (compatible with admin.doctor later)
- `bd store fsck --repair`: truncate corrupted tails, quarantine mid-file segments (move to quarantine/), rebuild wal.sqlite

**Acceptance**
- [ ] bd store fsck validates WAL segments offline
- [ ] --repair truncates tails and quarantines bad segments
- [ ] Report uses a stable FsckReport schema for later admin.doctor reuse
- [ ] Tests with golden corpus fixtures

**Files:** src/cli/commands/store.rs, src/daemon/wal/fsck.rs (new)
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 6: Legacy WAL snapshot migration import
description:
**Problem**
Existing users have data in legacy snapshot WAL format. Must import into new event WAL.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §17.1 (legacy WAL snapshot), §5.1 (rename to wal_legacy_snapshot.rs)

**Design**
- Rename src/daemon/wal.rs to src/daemon/wal_legacy_snapshot.rs
- On store open during migration: detect legacy snapshot WAL, read it, merge into StoreState["core"] namespace
- Proceed with new event WAL thereafter
- Keep legacy reader read-only for one release

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Legacy wal.rs renamed to wal_legacy_snapshot.rs
- [ ] Migration detects and imports legacy snapshot
- [ ] Data ends up in "core" namespace
- [ ] Tests verify migration path

**Files:** src/daemon/wal_legacy_snapshot.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/migration.rs (new)
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 6: Legacy Git ref migration import
description:
**Problem**
Existing users have data in refs/heads/beads/store. Must import into new checkpoint format.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §17.2 (legacy Git ref), §17.3 (identity migration)

**Design**
- On store open: check for refs/heads/beads/store
- Import into StoreState["core"] namespace using legacy wire format
- Leave checkpoint export to the scheduler (refs/beads/<store_id>/<group>) in bd-3m5.22
- Mirror legacy ref for one release if needed
- Keep legacy wire format reader for migration only

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Migration imports refs/heads/beads/store into StoreState["core"].
- [ ] Legacy ref is treated as read-only during migration.
- [ ] Identity persisted locally (URL changes don't break it).

darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 2: Core wire delta types
description:
**Problem**
Realtime events need a canonical delta representation that is not tied to the legacy git wire format. Today only src/git/wire.rs exists, so the realtime lane has no typed delta basis for EventBody and no canonical op ordering.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.7 (delta schema choice) and 2.4 (TxnDeltaV1 fields)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (WireBeadPatch, NotesPatch, TxnOpKey, TxnDeltaV1)

**Files:** src/core/wire_bead.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/git/wire.rs (reference only)
design:
**Design**
- Define core wire structs: WireBeadPatch, WireNoteV1, NotesPatch (Omitted vs AtLeast), NoteAppendV1, and TxnOpV1.
- Define TxnOpKey and represent TxnDeltaV1 as a canonical map keyed by TxnOpKey to enforce unique ops and stable ordering for hashing and encoding.
- Keep field names compatible with legacy wire to ease conversion, but keep types in core to avoid coupling realtime to git.
- Provide conversions between domain types (Bead, Note, Tombstone, DepEdge) and wire structs when lossless.
- Document notes rule: patches omit notes by default; if present they mean set union only (no truncation).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] serde roundtrip tests for new wire types.
- [ ] TxnDeltaV1 rejects duplicate op keys and preserves canonical ordering.
- [ ] Conversions to and from core domain types are covered for at least one bead and note case.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: Graceful shutdown + observability metrics
description:
**Problem**
Daemon must shut down gracefully without losing durable events. Operators need tracing and metrics.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (graceful shutdown, observability requirements)

**Design**
- On SIGTERM: stop accepting mutations, drain in-flight durability waits (bounded), flush WAL, close segments, exit
- Every mutation traceable via txn_id and client_request_id
- Replication sessions log peer identity and namespace authorization
- Emit counters: wal_append_ok/err, wal_fsync_ok/err, apply_ok/err, repl_events_in/out, checkpoint_export_ok/err
- Emit gauges: IPC inflight, repl queue depth, per-peer lag
- Emit histograms: wal_append_duration, wal_fsync_duration, apply_duration, checkpoint_duration

**Acceptance**
- [ ] SIGTERM triggers graceful drain and flush
- [ ] Metrics emitted via tracing or metrics crate
- [ ] Tests verify no event loss on shutdown
- [ ] Tests verify metric emission

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/metrics.rs (new), Cargo.toml (tracing/metrics)
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Cross-phase: Fuzz targets for WAL/CBOR/replication decode
description:
**Problem**
Plan requires fuzz testing for decode paths to find edge cases.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §18 (fuzzing + crash safety)

**Design**
- cargo-fuzz targets:
  - WAL frame decode (length/crc/tail truncation)
  - CBOR EventBody decode with bounds (depth, map entries, array entries)
  - Replication message decode (HELLO/WELCOME/EVENTS)
- Use libfuzzer or afl via cargo-fuzz
- CI runs fuzz for bounded time on each PR (optional)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] fuzz/fuzz_targets/wal_decode.rs exists
- [ ] fuzz/fuzz_targets/event_body_decode.rs exists
- [ ] fuzz/fuzz_targets/repl_message_decode.rs exists
- [ ] cargo fuzz run <target> works

**Files:** fuzz/fuzz_targets/wal_decode.rs (new), fuzz/fuzz_targets/event_body_decode.rs (new), fuzz/fuzz_targets/repl_message_decode.rs (new), Cargo.toml
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Cross-phase: Crash safety integration tests
description:
**Problem**
Plan requires crash tests to verify recovery behavior after SIGKILL at various points.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §18 (crash tests)

**Design**
- Integration tests that:
  - Inject SIGKILL during append (after write before fsync, after fsync before index commit, etc.)
  - Assert recovery: no duplicate origin_seq, tail truncation correct, index rebuild correct
- Use fork() or subprocess to inject crashes
- Verify idempotency mapping survives

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Test kills daemon mid-append and verifies recovery
- [ ] No duplicate origin_seq after crash
- [ ] Tail truncation handles partial records
- [ ] Index rebuild correct after crash

**Files:** tests/crash_recovery.rs (new)
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Cross-phase: Normative defaults constants + enforcement
description:
**Problem**
The realtime plan hardcodes many safety limits (bytes, events, timeouts). Today these values are scattered or absent; different subsystems could silently diverge, which breaks the single canonical behavior the plan expects.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19 (normative defaults + safety bounds) and §9.8 (flow control)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (Limits struct and defaults)

**Files:** src/core/limits.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/daemon/repl/*, src/daemon/wal/*, src/git/checkpoint/import.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs
design:
**Design**
- Define a single Limits struct in src/core/limits.rs with all normative defaults (frame sizes, WAL bounds, CBOR decode bounds, HLC drift, backpressure, checkpoint limits).
- Introduce small unit newtypes (Bytes, Events, Millis) or clearly name fields to avoid unit confusion across modules.
- Thread Limits through EventBody decode, WAL append, replication framing, gap buffers, and IPC inflight control.
- Make all limit enforcement explicit at module boundaries; failures return ErrorPayload(code="limit_exceeded") with details of which limit tripped.
- Allow overrides via config, but enforce that defaults are the plan values when no config is provided.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Limits struct covers every constant in REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19.
- [ ] Replication rejects oversize frames and batches at decode time.
- [ ] EventBody decoder rejects depth/map/array/byte-length violations.
- [ ] WAL append rejects records > MAX_WAL_RECORD_BYTES before write.
- [ ] Tests verify representative limits are enforced and surfaced as errors.

notes.content: Implemented Limits struct + defaults, config plumbing, IPC frame limit, WAL record size guard, and note/label limits with tests. Remaining for later phases: EventBody CBOR decode limits and replication frame/batch enforcement once those modules land.
notes.author: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 2: Canonical JSON encoder for checkpoints
description:
**Problem**
Checkpoint hashes require canonical JSON (sorted keys, no whitespace). serde_json doesn't guarantee key order.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.3 (canonical JSON normative)

**Design**
- Create src/core/json_canon.rs (or src/git/checkpoint/json_canon.rs)
- API: fn to_canon_json_bytes<T: Serialize>(v: &T) -> Vec<u8>
- Requirements:
  - Keys sorted by UTF-8 byte order ascending, recursively
  - No insignificant whitespace
  - No NaN/Infinity floats (reject if encountered)
  - Deterministic escaping
- Used by checkpoint export and manifest/meta writers

**Acceptance**
- [ ] to_canon_json_bytes produces deterministic output
- [ ] Keys sorted recursively
- [ ] Tests verify byte-for-byte reproducibility
- [ ] NaN/Infinity rejected

**Files:** src/core/json_canon.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/export.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: PeerAckTable for durability coordination
description:
**Problem**
ReplicatedFsync(k) requires a truthful view of which peers have durably ACKed which watermarks and head hashes. A naive map risks mixing namespaces or ignoring head hash divergence.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.12 (applied vs durable + head sha), §10.1-10.3 (durability coordination), §9.5 (ACK semantics)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (Watermarks<Durable>, HeadStatus)

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/peer_acks.rs (new), src/daemon/durability_coordinator.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- PeerAckTable tracks per peer: durable Watermarks<Durable>, optional durable_heads, and last_ack_at_ms.
- Enforce monotonicity on update: seq may only advance; head sha must match the seq (reject divergence).
- Provide queries: acked_by(ns, origin, seq) -> Vec<ReplicaId>, and satisfied_k(ns, origin, seq, k) -> bool.
- Maintain per-namespace eligibility via roster (durability_eligible); only count eligible peers.
- Surface divergence: if a peer reports the same seq but a different head sha, mark that peer as diverged and exclude from durability quorum.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] PeerAckTable stores per-peer durable/applied watermarks and heads with monotonic updates.
- [ ] Divergent head sha at same seq is detected and excluded from eligibility.
- [ ] satisfied_k computes k-of-n for eligible peers with explicit failure cases.
- [ ] Unit tests cover monotonic updates and satisfied_k behavior.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: Replication session state machine
description:
**Problem**
bd-3m5.17 is too large. Split: session state machine handles single connection lifecycle.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.3-9.4 (handshake, EVENTS processing)
- Refines bd-3m5.17

**Design**
- Implement Session struct in src/daemon/repl/session.rs

darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 2: EventBody canonical CBOR + hashing
description:
**Problem**
Realtime WAL and replication require a canonical EventBody representation whose bytes are the hash preimage. There is no EventBody type or canonical encoding path in core yet, so we cannot guarantee stable hashing or verify remote frames.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.6 (canonical CBOR + hash over EventBody bytes), 2.4 (EventBody fields), 9.4 (EVENTS frame)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (EventBytes, EventFrameV1, VerifiedEvent)

**Files:** src/core/event.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, Cargo.toml (minicbor), tests under src/core
design:
**Design**
- Define EventBody, EventKindV1, and EventId in src/core/event.rs with StoreIdentity and NamespaceId baked in.
- Add EventBytes<Canonical> and EventBytes<Opaque> wrappers to separate locally authored canonical bytes from opaque remote bytes.
- Implement canonical CBOR encoder for EventBody using minicbor with definite lengths and sorted map keys for hashed types; reject floats and indefinite lengths.
- Provide helpers: encode_event_body_canonical -> EventBytes<Canonical>, hash_event_body -> Sha256.
- Add decode with strict bounds (depth, map entries, byte length) and return EventBytes<Opaque> + EventBody.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canonical encode produces stable bytes and hashes across runs (unit tests).
- [ ] Decoder rejects indefinite length and out of bounds payloads.
- [ ] EventBytes typestate is used in frame or WAL APIs to prevent hash preimage ambiguity.

darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: IPC namespace + durability + client_request_id fields
description:
**Problem**
IPC currently has no namespace, durability class, or client_request_id fields, and defaults are implemented ad hoc in CLI/daemon. This makes retries and read-your-writes semantics ambiguous and invites mismatched defaults across entry points.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (IPC evolution) and §16.1 (request fields and defaults)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (NamespaceId, DurabilityClass, Seq0/Seq1)

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/api/mod.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/*, src/daemon/query.rs
design:
**Design**
- Extend mutation request structs with optional fields: namespace, durability, client_request_id, actor_id, preserving backward compatibility.
- Parse namespace into NamespaceId and client_request_id into ClientRequestId early, so validation happens before mutation planning.
- Centralize defaulting rules in one place (e.g., Request::normalize): namespace defaults to core, durability defaults to LocalFsync, actor_id defaults to daemon actor.
- Add read/query gating fields require_min_seen and wait_timeout_ms; require_min_seen uses applied watermarks and returns retryable timeout error on miss.
- Ensure IPC JSON schema stays stable: new fields are optional and omitted in serialization when None.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Mutation request structs add optional namespace/durability/client_request_id/actor_id with backward-compatible defaults.
- [ ] Read/query request structs add optional require_min_seen + wait_timeout_ms with consistent defaulting.
- [ ] CLI flags --namespace, --durability, --client-request-id map to IPC fields.
- [ ] Defaulting (namespace=core, durability=LocalFsync, actor_id=daemon default) is applied consistently.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 1: DurabilityReceipt response types
description:
**Problem**
Receipts are the durable contract for retries and read-your-writes. Today there is no shared receipt type, so IPC responses risk being inconsistent and partial, and errors cannot safely return a receipt for retry.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11 (idempotency mapping + receipts), §10.5 (receipt semantics), §16.1 (IPC response shape)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (DurabilityProofV1, DurabilityOutcome)

**Files:** src/core/durability.rs, src/api/mod.rs

**Design**
- Define DurabilityProofV1 to capture the local fsync proof and optional replication acks.
- Define DurabilityOutcome (Achieved vs Pending) to avoid ambiguous Option<DurabilityClass> fields.
- Define DurabilityReceipt { store: StoreIdentity, txn_id, event_ids, durability_proof, outcome, min_seen } with serde support.
- Keep types serde-friendly for IPC JSON; daemon/IPC wiring lives in bd-3m5.14.
design:
**Design**
- Define DurabilityProofV1 to capture the local fsync proof and optional replication acks.
- Define DurabilityOutcome (Achieved vs Pending) to avoid ambiguous Option<DurabilityClass> fields.
- Define DurabilityReceipt { store: StoreIdentity, txn_id, event_ids, durability_proof, outcome, min_seen } with serde support.
- Ensure idempotent retry returns the same txn_id/event_ids, with monotonic improvement of durability_proof if new ACKs are observed.
- IPC success returns receipt; timeout returns retryable error with receipt included.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] DurabilityProofV1 and DurabilityOutcome types match REALTIME_PLAN.md semantics (local fsync + optional peer acks; pending vs achieved).
- [ ] DurabilityReceipt includes store identity, txn_id, event_ids, durability_proof, outcome, and min_seen.
- [ ] Serde JSON roundtrip tests cover DurabilityReceipt and DurabilityProofV1.
- [ ] No daemon/IPC behavior changes in this bead (core types only).

title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 2: core apply_event for EventBody
description:
**Problem**
The daemon mutates CanonicalState directly, so apply semantics are scattered and can diverge across mutation and replication paths. We need a single pure apply_event path that deterministically maps EventBody deltas to CRDT joins.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.8 (deterministic apply), 2.4 (TxnDeltaV1), 8.3 (apply pipeline)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (TxnOpV1, TxnDeltaV1)

**Files:** src/core/apply.rs (new), src/core/state.rs, src/core/bead.rs, src/core/dep.rs, src/core/tombstone.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add core::apply_event(&mut CanonicalState, &EventBody) -> ApplyOutcome as a pure function with no I/O.
- Apply TxnDeltaV1 ops in canonical op key order so repeated application is deterministic.
- Use existing CRDT joins (Lww, Tombstone::join, DepEdge::join) and preserve tombstone resurrection rules.
- Detect note id collisions with mismatched content as corruption (return error), and treat duplicate note append as idempotent.
- Return ApplyOutcome with changed bead ids, dep keys, and note ids to drive indexing and checkpoint dirtiness.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] apply_event is deterministic and idempotent on repeated events (tests).
- [ ] Note id collision with different content is detected and surfaced.
- [ ] ApplyOutcome reports changes used by indexing and checkpoint scheduling.
