darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Centralize dependency spec parsing
description:
**Problem**
Dependency spec parsing is duplicated in `src/cli/mod.rs` (`normalize_dep_specs`) and `src/daemon/executor.rs` (`parse_dep_specs`). The two implementations can drift (string format, DepKind parsing, error messages), which makes CLI validation diverge from daemon validation and complicates reasoning about dependency formats.

**Design**
Add a shared parser in core and use it from both CLI and daemon:
- Introduce a `DepSpec` type in `src/core/dep.rs` (or a small `dep_spec` module) with `{ kind: DepKind, id: BeadId }`.
- Implement `DepSpec::parse_list(raw: &[String]) -> Result<Vec<DepSpec>, CoreError>` that handles comma-separated lists and optional `kind:id` prefixes.
- Implement `DepSpec::to_spec_string()` for canonical formatting (`blocks:bd-123` or `bd-123` for default).
- Update `src/cli/mod.rs` to replace `normalize_dep_specs` with core parsing + `to_spec_string()` for normalized request strings.
- Update `src/daemon/executor.rs` to replace its local `parse_dep_specs` with core parsing and map errors to `OpError::ValidationFailed`.

**Design Notes**
Keep the current user-facing behavior (comma-separated specs, optional `kind:` prefix) and preserve existing error messages as much as possible. Use `DepKind::parse` and `BeadId::parse` in one place so the spec grammar is canonical.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] CLI and daemon both rely on the core parser for dependency specs.
- [ ] Canonical formatting is unchanged for default `blocks` dependencies.
- [ ] Error messages remain user-friendly and point to the `deps` field.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/core/dep.rs`, `src/cli/mod.rs`, `src/daemon/executor.rs`
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Unify CLI description/body normalization
description:
**Problem**
`resolve_description` is duplicated in `src/cli/commands/create.rs` and `src/cli/commands/update.rs`. The duplicated logic increases drift risk (different error messages or behavior) and makes it harder to reason about CLI validation.

**Design**
Extract a shared helper into `src/cli/mod.rs` (or `src/cli/commands/mod.rs`) that resolves `--description` and `--body`:
- Add `fn resolve_description(description: Option<String>, body: Option<String>) -> Result<Option<String>>` returning the same validation error as today.
- Replace both call sites in create/update with the shared helper.
- Keep error messages identical and ensure the helper stays CLI-only (no daemon changes).

**Design Notes**
This is a pure refactor: no behavior changes, just a single source of truth for the validation error and normalization.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `create` and `update` use the shared helper; duplicate implementations removed.
- [ ] Error messages and behavior are unchanged from the current CLI.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/cli/mod.rs`, `src/cli/commands/create.rs`, `src/cli/commands/update.rs`
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Move label/parent updates into daemon ops
description:
**Problem**
CLI handlers perform read-modify-write for labels and parent relationships. `src/cli/commands/label.rs` fetches each issue then sends a full `Update` patch, and `src/cli/commands/update.rs` queries deps then removes/adds parent edges. This makes changes non-atomic, adds round trips, and splits core behavior across CLI and daemon.

**Design**
Add daemon-side ops for label and parent mutations and switch CLI to use them:
- Extend IPC `Request` with `AddLabels { repo, id, labels: Vec<String> }` and `RemoveLabels { repo, id, labels: Vec<String> }`, or a single `UpdateLabels { add, remove }` request.
- Add `SetParent { repo, id, parent: Option<String> }` that replaces existing parent edges in one mutation.
- Implement handlers in `src/daemon/executor.rs` that apply the changes atomically in a single WAL mutation, validating label formats and parent existence.
- Update CLI `label` commands to call the new label ops; update `update` to call `SetParent` when `--parent/--no-parent` is provided.
- Keep response payloads consistent (use `OpResult::Updated` or add a small label/parent result if needed for clarity).

**Design Notes**
This keeps CLI thin and centralizes behavior in the daemon. It also avoids subtle races (labels changed between fetch and update) and makes parent updates atomic.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] CLI label add/remove no longer fetches issues; daemon applies label changes atomically.
- [ ] Parent updates are handled by a dedicated daemon op; CLI no longer manipulates deps directly.
- [ ] Label validation errors and missing parent errors remain user-friendly.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/ipc.rs`, `src/daemon/ops.rs`, `src/daemon/executor.rs`, `src/cli/commands/label.rs`, `src/cli/commands/update.rs`, `src/api/mod.rs`
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Move BeadPatch application into BeadPatch helper
description:
**Problem**
`Daemon::apply_update` applies `BeadPatch` field-by-field inline in `src/daemon/executor.rs`. The patch semantics live in `src/daemon/ops.rs`, but the actual application logic lives elsewhere, so adding or changing fields requires touching multiple places and is easy to miss.

**Design**
Add a helper on `BeadPatch` to apply patches directly to `BeadFields`:
- Implement `BeadPatch::apply_to_fields(&self, fields: &mut BeadFields, stamp: &Stamp) -> Result<(), OpError>` in `src/daemon/ops.rs`.
- Move the current field-application logic (including label parsing and status -> workflow transition) into this helper.
- Update `Daemon::apply_update` to call the helper inside the WAL mutation.

**Design Notes**
Keep behavior identical: required fields cannot be cleared, label parsing stays strict, and status strings map to the same workflows with the same validation errors.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `Daemon::apply_update` delegates patch application to `BeadPatch::apply_to_fields`.
- [ ] Label validation and workflow transitions behave exactly as before.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/ops.rs`, `src/daemon/executor.rs`
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Consolidate CLI Filters building
description:
**Problem**
`handle_list` and `handle_count` both build `Filters` manually with overlapping fields and similar trimming logic. The duplication makes it easy for behavior to drift (e.g., title/label handling) and adds cognitive overhead when updating filter semantics.

**Design**
Introduce a small CLI helper to construct `Filters`:
- Add a helper (e.g., `filters_from_list_args`, `filters_from_count_args`, or a `FiltersBuilder`) in `src/cli/mod.rs` or a new `src/cli/filters.rs`.
- Move common normalization (status, priority, bead_type, assignee, labels, search text, parent) into shared code.
- Keep command-specific fields (e.g., time ranges for count) in their respective handlers.

**Design Notes**
This is a refactor only; do not change behavior. Preserve exact parsing and validation error messages.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `handle_list` and `handle_count` use shared `Filters` construction for common fields.

darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Remove unused BeadOp / Request::to_op duplication
description:
**Problem**
`BeadOp` and `Request::to_op`/`to_query` duplicate the IPC field definitions but are not used by the runtime path (`Daemon::handle_request` does its own parsing). This duplication adds surface area without benefit and makes it harder to reason about which path is authoritative.

**Design**
Simplify the IPC/ops surface by removing unused conversion helpers:
- Audit call sites to confirm `BeadOp` and `Request::to_op`/`to_query` are unused outside tests.
- Remove `Request::to_op`/`to_query` and the `BeadOp` type if unused; update tests accordingly.
- If external API concerns exist, replace with a small internal helper or mark the methods `#[deprecated]` and keep a single authoritative path (`handle_request`).

**Design Notes**
Goal is to reduce duplication and keep one clear entry point for validation and parsing.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Unused `BeadOp`/`Request::to_op`/`to_query` code is removed or deprecated with no functional change.
- [ ] IPC request handling continues to use a single authoritative parsing path.

darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Encode event/patch invariants in types (validated EventBody/TxnDelta)
description:
**Problem**
Core mutation accepts raw wire/event types that can represent invalid states. `apply_event` takes `EventBody` (raw) and `TxnOpV1`/`WireBeadPatch` without a validated type boundary. We rely on runtime validators (`validate_event_body_semantics`, `workflow_patch_from_wire`, `claim_patch_from_wire`, partial checks in `apply_event`) and on call sites remembering to run them. That is a compiler blind spot: a new ingestion path, refactor, or test helper can bypass validation and still mutate CanonicalState.

Examples of illegal states that compile today:
- `WireBeadPatch`: `status=Closed` with `closed_reason=Keep` or `closed_on_branch=Keep`.
- Claim patch where `assignee`/`assignee_expires` are mismatched or partially set.
- `created_at` set without `created_by` (or vice versa).
- Self-dependencies in `DepAdd`/`DepRemove`.
- Tombstone lineage fields partially set or invalid.

This violates “types should tell the truth” and “parse, don’t validate”. Invalid states are representable *after* parsing and can flow into core logic.

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-core/src/apply.rs:51` — `apply_event` accepts raw `EventBody`.
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs:2353` — runtime semantic validation.
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs:2418` — `ValidatedBeadPatch` exists but isn’t the boundary.

**Impact**
Deterministic corruption of canonical state from unvalidated events. This is a P0 correctness risk because it weakens the CRDT safety boundary and makes refactors dangerous.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Make validation a *type boundary*, not a convention.

1) Split raw vs validated types:
- Rename current `EventBody` to `EventBodyRaw` (private to module) or wrap it in `EventBody<Unvalidated>`.
- Define `EventBody<Validated>` (or `ValidatedEventBody`) that is the only type accepted by core apply.
- Mirror this for `TxnDelta` and `TxnOpV1` if needed, or wrap `TxnDeltaV1` inside a validated event type.

2) Make validation constructors explicit:
- `impl TryFrom<EventBodyRaw> for EventBody<Validated>` runs *all* semantic + limits validation.
- `ValidatedBeadPatch` should be the only patch type that reaches `apply_bead_upsert`.
- Add `ValidatedDepAdd`, `ValidatedDepRemove`, `ValidatedTombstone` wrappers if needed to encode per-op invariants.

3) Narrow core API surface:
- Change `apply_event(state, body)` to accept only `EventBody<Validated>`.
- Make `apply_bead_upsert` accept `ValidatedBeadPatch` (not `WireBeadPatch`).
- Provide a single validation boundary in ingestion, e.g. `verify_event_frame` returns `VerifiedEvent<Validated>`.

4) Parse-don’t-validate rule:
- Public decode APIs should return validated types or error (no raw types escaping).
- Raw/unchecked constructors are `pub(crate)` or private.

5) Migration plan:
- Add new types and conversions, then update all call sites.
- Temporarily keep deprecated wrappers that call validation; remove them once all callers are migrated.

This makes “forgetting to validate” a compile error.
acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] `apply_event` only accepts a validated event type; raw `EventBody` cannot compile at call sites.
- [ ] `apply_bead_upsert`/dep operations require validated patch/op wrappers, not raw wire structs.
- [ ] There is exactly one public validation boundary from wire bytes to validated events; raw decode types do not escape the module.
- [ ] All existing callers are migrated; no `validate_event_body_*` calls remain outside the boundary.
- [ ] Tests: at least one compile‑fail or trybuild test demonstrating that invalid patches cannot reach core apply; runtime tests still reject invalid events at the boundary.

darin@book
title: darin@book
Deprecate legacy snapshot WAL and remove from runtime path
description:
**Problem**
Legacy snapshot WAL (`wal_legacy_snapshot`) and `apply_wal_mutation` are still wired into the daemon runtime even though realtime WAL + replication are in place. The legacy WAL is initialized on daemon start and the code path remains in `Daemon` despite not being used by the new mutation pipeline. This keeps two durability systems alive, increases cognitive load, and makes future changes riskier.

**Signals / Evidence**
- Daemon still constructs legacy `Wal` in `run_daemon` (`src/daemon/run.rs`).
- `Daemon` stores `wal: Arc<Wal>` and exposes `apply_wal_mutation` even though new mutations use event WAL (`src/daemon/core.rs`).
- `apply_wal_mutation` is only referenced in tests (`src/daemon/core.rs`).

**Why this hurts velocity**
Two durability paths mean double mental model and duplicated invariants. It’s easy to accidentally keep legacy behavior alive or regress realtime expectations.
design:
**Design**
- Gate legacy WAL behind a feature flag or explicit migration mode.
- Remove `apply_wal_mutation` from the main execution path (keep only for legacy migration tests if needed).
- If realtime is stable, remove the legacy WAL from daemon startup entirely and leave migration in a separate tool/module.
- Document the deprecation timeline and add a kill switch if we need emergency fallback.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Legacy snapshot WAL is not initialized in the normal runtime path.
- [ ] `apply_wal_mutation` is removed or clearly isolated to legacy/migration mode.
- [ ] No production code depends on legacy WAL once realtime is verified.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
WAL read/merge failures must not be ignored
description:
**Problem**
`ensure_repo_loaded` ignores WAL read errors and WAL merge errors (`if let Ok(Some(wal_entry))` + warn on `join` failure). Corrupted WAL or merge conflicts can silently drop unsynced data.

**Design**
Treat WAL read/merge failures as fatal for load: return an OpError that surfaces the WAL issue, keep the WAL file intact, and avoid overwriting state with stale git data. Add a test that injects a corrupted WAL and asserts load returns an error.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] WAL read errors are returned to the caller (no silent ignore)
- [ ] WAL merge conflicts return a structured error
- [ ] WAL file is preserved on error
- [ ] Tests cover corrupted WAL and merge failure paths
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/wal.rs, src/daemon/ops.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Make dependency ops carry non-self DepKey in wire types
description:
**Problem**
Dependency self-edges are only prevented by runtime validation (`validate_event_body_semantics` checks `from == to`). Yet the domain already has `DepKey::new` which makes self-deps impossible. Wire ops (`WireDepAddV1`/`WireDepRemoveV1`) still carry raw `from/to/kind`, so invalid states are representable until runtime. This makes it easy for new codepaths to forget validation and introduce self-deps, and it violates the “information holds its shape” / “types tell the truth” principles.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-core/src/dep.rs:52` — `DepKey::new` enforces non-self.
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs:2371` — runtime self-dep check.
- `crates/beads-core/src/wire_bead.rs:683` — wire dep ops store raw `from/to/kind`.

Severity: Self-deps are logically invalid and can cause cycle detection/path logic to misbehave. Relying on runtime checks here is fragile and non-local.
design:
**Design**
Represent dependency operations in terms of `DepKey` (or a `NonSelfDepKey` newtype) so self-deps are unrepresentable.

1) Introduce `NonSelfDepKey(DepKey)` (or reuse `DepKey` directly) with serde that preserves the existing wire shape `{from,to,kind}`.
2) Update `WireDepAddV1`/`WireDepRemoveV1` to store `key: NonSelfDepKey` and expose accessors for `from/to/kind`.
3) Provide serde impls that encode/decode to the same JSON layout to maintain compatibility.
4) Update `TxnOpV1::key()` and any dep handling to use the new key field.
5) Remove the self-dep check in `validate_event_body_semantics` once it’s structurally impossible, leaving only the tombstone lineage validation.

Migration notes:
- If serde compatibility is risky, add `#[serde(flatten)]` with a custom proxy to preserve wire form.
- Add a small conversion layer for old tests/fixtures if needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] It is impossible to construct a dep add/remove op with `from == to` without a fallible conversion.
- [ ] Wire serialization remains backward-compatible (same JSON fields).
- [ ] `validate_event_body_semantics` no longer needs a self-dep check.
- [ ] Existing dep-related tests pass; add at least one compile-time or conversion test for self-dep rejection.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local

darin@book
title: darin@book
Enforce protocol range invariants in type
description:
**Problem**
`ProtocolRange::new(min, max)` does not enforce `min <= max`. This allows constructing invalid ranges that only fail later at negotiation. It’s a typestate gap: the type claims to be a range but permits impossible states.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs:44` — `ProtocolRange` + `new`.
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs:71` — `SessionConfig::new` constructs ranges.

**Design**
Encode the invariant in the type.

- Make `ProtocolRange::new` return `Result<Self, ProtocolRangeError>` and validate `min <= max`.
- Add helpers: `ProtocolRange::exact(v)` and `ProtocolRange::range(min, max)`.
- Consider `NonZeroU32` for versions if 0 is invalid.
- Update session config construction and any tests to use the validated constructor.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] It is impossible to construct a `ProtocolRange` with `min > max`.
- [ ] All call sites use `ProtocolRange::exact` or validated `new`.
- [ ] Tests cover invalid ranges (min>max) and valid exact-range negotiation.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs`

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Stateright model: pending ingest contiguity counterexample
description:
**Problem**
Running `cargo run --example session_coordinator_boundary -- check` reports a counterexample for the property "pending ingest starts at next expected". The trace shows pending ingest can start ahead of the next expected seq:
- DeliverEvent(3)
- DeliverEvent(2)
- DeliverEvent(1)
- CoordinatorIngest
- DeliverEvent(1)
- DeliverEvent(2)
- DeliverAck

This indicates either the model invariant is wrong or the coordinator boundary logic allows a gap to slip through.

**Design**
Inspect `beads_stateright_models/examples/session_coordinator_boundary.rs` and the underlying boundary logic. Confirm the intended rule (pending ingest should start at next expected, not beyond). If the rule is intended, adjust the model and/or coordinator state machine so it enforces contiguity when ingesting pending events. Add a regression test in the beads runtime to mirror the counterexample trace once the implementation exists (or add a model-only regression property if implementation work is not yet wired).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Model check passes without the "pending ingest starts at next expected" counterexample.
- [ ] If rule is intended, coordinator boundary enforces contiguity (no ingest beyond next expected).
- [ ] Regression test added for the counterexample trace (model or implementation).

**Files:** beads_stateright_models/examples/session_coordinator_boundary.rs

CRDT Overhaul audit fixes + verification

**Problem**
Post-audit fixes and verification work for CRDT overhaul issues identified in bd-ogvs.*.

**Scope**

**Files**: src/core, src/git, src/daemon, tests/*, CRDT_OVERHAUL.md

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
OR-Set DVV pruning removes unrelated values (fix ORSWOT semantics)
description:
**Problem**
OrSet uses Dvv as max-per-replica and prunes any dot dominated by cc. A remove of one value advances cc and incorrectly removes unrelated values added earlier by the same replica. This breaks labels/deps semantics (remove one label can erase others).

**Files**: src/core/orset.rs, src/core/state.rs, src/core/event.rs, src/git/wire.rs, src/daemon/wal_legacy_snapshot.rs
design:
**Design**
Implement correct ORSWOT removal semantics by making Dvv a dotted version vector (max + dot exceptions) or equivalent tombstone mechanism so removal can target specific dots without wiping earlier dots from the same replica.

Implementation sketch:
- Extend Dvv to include an explicit dot set (exception set).
- dominates(dot) returns true if dot.counter <= max[replica] OR dot in dots.
- merge/join merges max and dot sets and compacts dots covered by max.
- apply_remove(value, ctx) removes dots for that value dominated by ctx and merges ctx into cc (without over-approximating unrelated dots).
- Update encode/decode for DVV if representation changes (wire + git + WAL).
- Add normalization to drop empty entries and resolve collisions deterministically after join.

Testing should focus on remove targeting only intended dots and add-wins behavior under concurrency.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Unit test: same replica adds two values, remove one -> other survives.
- [ ] Unit test: apply_remove with ctx containing dot for value A does NOT remove value B from same replica.
- [ ] Join test: a has value A, b removes B and advances ctx; join preserves A.
- [ ] Join test: concurrent add vs remove (ctx not covering new dot) is add-wins.
- [ ] Serialization roundtrip preserves DVV semantics (encode/decode for DVV if format changed).

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
WAL snapshot format transition: decide compat vs explicit failure
description:
**Problem**
Recent WAL snapshots may not deserialize after WalDepStore format change. The current untagged enum will fail without a clear remediation path.

**Files**: src/daemon/wal_legacy_snapshot.rs
design:
**Design**
Pick and implement one strategy:
A) Add a transitional legacy variant that can parse the previous DepStore-shaped WAL JSON and convert to DepStore.
B) If backward compatibility is explicitly unsupported, detect the shape and return a clear error instructing users to delete old WAL files.

Include tests for the chosen strategy using a handcrafted JSON sample of the prior format (or a simulated struct) to ensure behavior stays locked.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Test: legacy WAL sample either deserializes correctly (compat path) or fails with explicit wipe guidance (non-compat path).\n- [ ] Test: current WalEntry still roundtrips.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Deterministic merge for bead/note collisions in CanonicalState::join
description:
**Problem**
CanonicalState::join and NoteStore::join are left-biased for collisions, so merges can be non-commutative when collisions exist. This violates the D4 deterministic convergence goal.

**Files**: src/core/state.rs, src/core/bead.rs, src/core/apply.rs, tests/integration/core/apply.rs
design:
**Design**
Implement collision resolution in join using the same comparator as apply:
- Bead collision winner: (created_stamp, content_hash) per CRDT_OVERHAUL.md. Insert lineage tombstone for loser.
- Note collision winner: (note.at, note.author, sha256(content)).
Update NoteStore::join to choose winner deterministically, not left-biased.
Ensure join is commutative and idempotent even with collisions in inputs.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Unit/integration test: two bead creations with same id in different orders yield same winner + lineage tombstone.\n- [ ] Unit test: NoteStore::join resolves collisions deterministically, order-independent.\n- [ ] Property test: join is commutative for states with collisions.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Determinism tests: vary label/dep stamps across insertion order
description:
**Problem**
Current determinism tests use identical stamps; they may miss order-dependent stamp regressions.

**Files**: src/git/wire.rs, src/core/state.rs
design: **Design**\nExtend determinism tests to use different stamps for label/dep ops and apply them in opposite orders, asserting identical serialized output and updated_stamp behavior.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Test: label ops with different stamps applied in reversed order serialize identically.\n- [ ] Test: dep ops with different stamps applied in reversed order serialize identically.

description: darin@book

**Files**: src/core/apply.rs, tests/integration/core/apply.rs
design: **Design**\nAdd integration tests that apply LabelAdd/Remove, DepAdd/Remove, and NoteAppend to missing beads, then create the bead and verify the ops become visible.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Tests: orphan label/dep/note ops do not error on apply.\n- [ ] Tests: after later bead creation, labels/notes/deps become visible via queries.

- Add regression test: dep add -> remove -> re-add results in membership and indexes reflecting the re-added edge.
- Include dependency-specific tests as requested.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Invariant test: dep_indexes contains exactly dep_store.values().\n- [ ] Regression test: delete then re-add a dep restores membership and indexes.\n- [ ] Dependency tests cover both outgoing and incoming index paths.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Legacy deps parsing tests (git wire)

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Cleanup: remove/justify ApplyError collision variants
description:
**Problem**
ApplyError still contains BeadCollision/NoteCollision variants even though collisions are now meant to be deterministic and total. This contradicts the CRDT overhaul goal and leaves dead/error paths.

**Files**: src/core/apply.rs, src/core/state.rs
design: **Design**\nAudit remaining call sites for BeadCollision/NoteCollision. If collisions are fully handled deterministically, remove the variants and refactor callers to be infallible (or treat as internal invariants). If still needed, document why and align with CRDT_OVERHAUL.md.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] No reachable code path returns ApplyError::{BeadCollision, NoteCollision} OR clear docs justify remaining use.\n- [ ] Tests updated to match the chosen approach.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Tombstone lineage pairing: make invalid lineage state unrepresentable
description:
**Problem**
encode_wire_tombstone can emit invalid CBOR if exactly one lineage field is set; debug_assert is skipped in release. This risks corrupt WAL/replication frames.

**Files**: src/core/event.rs, src/core/wire_bead.rs, src/git/checkpoint/*
design:
**Design**
Make tombstone lineage pairing a typestate invariant. Replace the two Option fields with a single Option<LineageStamp> (struct containing at+by). Encode only when lineage is Some. Add runtime validation that rejects partial lineage in decode or construction paths.

Also add a semantic validator hook for tombstones so invalid lineage can never reach encoding.

Update wire conversion helpers (checkpoint import/export) to use the new lineage representation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Unit test: encoding a tombstone with lineage=None produces correct map length.
- [ ] Unit test: decoding a tombstone with only one lineage field returns a validation error.
- [ ] Roundtrip test: tombstone with lineage Some(at,by) encodes and decodes identically.
- [ ] Regression test: validate_event_body rejects tombstone with partial lineage before encoding.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Cleanup: remove orset.rs dependency on event::Sha256
description:
**Problem**
orset.rs depends on event::Sha256 even though it only uses a placeholder op_hash arg. This is a layer violation risk as event depends on OR-Set elsewhere.

**Files**: src/core/orset.rs
design: **Design**\nRemove the dependency by either: (a) dropping the unused op_hash parameter, (b) accepting raw [u8;32], or (c) defining a lower-layer hash type. Ensure no cyclic dependency is created.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] orset.rs no longer imports event::Sha256.\n- [ ] Public API remains stable or is migrated cleanly with tests updated.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Cleanup: update CRDT_OVERHAUL.md wire format details
description:
**Problem**
CRDT_OVERHAUL.md describes DVV and dot encoding shapes that differ from the current implementation (map vs vec, field names).

**Files**: CRDT_OVERHAUL.md
design: **Design**\nUpdate docs to match actual on-wire encoding (CBOR/json) for WireDvvV1/WireDotV1 and any other drift discovered. Keep spec statements aligned with code to avoid future mismatches.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] CRDT_OVERHAUL.md updated to reflect current wire encoding.\n- [ ] Docs explicitly call out map vs vec and field names used in CBOR.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Cleanup: Labels::remove return value should reflect change
description:
**Problem**
Labels::remove returns true unconditionally, which can hide bugs at call sites and mislead tests.

**Files**: src/core/collections.rs
design: **Design**\nEither return a real changed flag (before_len != after_len) or change the API to return () and update call sites. Pick whichever matches usage patterns best.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Labels::remove no longer returns a misleading unconditional true.\n- [ ] Call sites updated to the chosen API.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Cleanup: DRY WalDepStore vs WireDepStoreV1
description:
**Problem**
WalDepStore duplicates the structure of WireDepStoreV1, increasing maintenance burden and risk of drift.

**Files**: src/daemon/wal_legacy_snapshot.rs, src/core/wire_bead.rs (or shared module)
design: **Design**\nConsider sharing a common internal dep-store struct or reusing WireDepStoreV1 if layering allows. Ensure serialization remains stable and test coverage stays intact.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] WalDepStore and wire dep store use shared representation or documented reasons for divergence.\n- [ ] Tests cover WAL dep serialization after refactor.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Resolve note collision policy mismatch (REALTIME_PLAN vs CRDT_OVERHAUL)
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN.md says note-id collisions with different content are corruption (not LWW), but CRDT_OVERHAUL.md and current code resolve note collisions deterministically. This spec mismatch can confuse future changes and tests.

**Design**
Pick the authoritative policy and align docs/tests accordingly:
- If corruption: update validation/apply to reject note-id collisions and adjust tests.
- If deterministic: update REALTIME_PLAN.md wording to match CRDT_OVERHAUL.md and keep tests.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Spec text is consistent across REALTIME_PLAN.md and CRDT_OVERHAUL.md.
- [ ] Tests match the chosen policy.

**Files**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md
- CRDT_OVERHAUL.md
- docs/CRDT_AUDIT.md (if referenced)
darin@book
title: darin@book
Label/dep stamp monotonicity + stamp on internal OR-Set change
description:
**Problem**
LabelState.stamp (and DepStore.stamp) is assigned directly on membership change; out-of-order apply can regress stamps and updated_stamp. Also, OR-Set internal changes (new dot, cc merge) can occur without membership change but currently do not update stamps.

**Files**: src/core/state.rs, src/core/orset.rs, src/core/bead.rs, src/git/wire.rs
design:
**Design**
- Use max(existing, incoming) for stamp updates to enforce monotonicity.
- Extend OrSetChange to indicate internal state mutation (not only membership diff).
- Update apply_label_add/remove and apply_dep_add/remove to update stamps when internal OR-Set state changes, not just membership diff.

Keep updated_stamp behavior aligned with CRDT_OVERHAUL.md (labels/notes/fields max).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Unit test: apply two label ops out of order; label stamp remains max.
- [ ] Unit test: adding a new dot for an existing label updates label stamp even if membership unchanged.
- [ ] Determinism test: apply same label/dep ops in different orders with different stamps => identical serialized state.
- [ ] Dep stamp behavior mirrors label stamp behavior in tests.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Meta.json corruption must fail load + checksum verification
description:
**Problem**
read_state_at_oid ignores meta.json if parsing fails, silently skipping checksum verification even though meta.json exists. This violates spec and masks corruption.

**Files**: src/git/sync.rs, src/git/wire.rs
design:
**Design**
- If meta.json exists and parse_meta fails, return a SyncError (do not load state).
- If parse_meta succeeds and checksums are present, always verify.
- Preserve existing behavior only when meta.json is absent.

Add focused tests in sync to cover corrupted meta.json and checksum mismatch behavior.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Test: meta.json present but invalid -> read_state_at_oid returns error.\n- [ ] Test: meta.json present with checksums -> checksum mismatch returns error.\n- [ ] Test: meta.json absent -> load succeeds (legacy behavior).

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Fix sample_bead_patch fixture to satisfy D1 workflow rules
description:
**Problem**
sample_bead_patch sets status=Open but leaves closed_reason/closed_on_branch as Keep, which violates D1 validation and will fail any path that validates events.

**Files**: tests/integration/fixtures/event_body.rs
design: **Design**\nUpdate fixture to explicitly Clear closed_reason and closed_on_branch when status is set, or omit status entirely depending on intent. Add a small fixture validation test that validate_event_body accepts the generated sample event.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Fixture uses explicit closure fields when status is set (or status omitted).\n- [ ] New test: validate_event_body accepts sample_event_body.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
WalDepStore conversion must not create empty-dot entries
description:
**Problem**
WalDepStore::into_dep_store inserts entries with empty dot sets, which violates OrSet invariants and can create phantom membership.

**Files**: src/daemon/wal_legacy_snapshot.rs, src/core/orset.rs
design: **Design**\nSkip entries with empty dot sets during conversion, or normalize through OrSet constructor that prunes empties. Ensure resulting DepStore contains only keys with non-empty dot sets. Add a regression test with empty-dot entries.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Unit test: WalDepStore with empty dots produces DepStore with no values.\n- [ ] Unit test: WalDepStore with mixed empty/non-empty dots preserves only non-empty entries.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Claim/workflow patch validation: tighten rules + typestate patch types
description:
**Problem**
Claim validation only checks Keep mismatch; it allows explicit but nonsensical combos (e.g., assignee Clear with expires Set). Workflow/claim patches can be constructed in invalid states, then only fail at apply or produce odd semantics.

**Files**: src/core/event.rs, src/core/apply.rs, src/core/wire_bead.rs, src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tests: invalid claim combos (Clear+Set, Set+Clear where disallowed) are rejected by validation.
- [ ] Tests: valid claim combos are accepted and apply deterministically.
- [ ] Tests: workflow status set requires explicit closure fields (already validated).
- [ ] Encoder/mutation_engine only accepts validated patch types.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
DepKind canonical ordering for deterministic deps.jsonl
description:
**Problem**
DepKind derives Ord by enum order. If spec expects lexical ordering, deps.jsonl sort order may be ambiguous or inconsistent across implementations.

**Files**: src/core/domain.rs, src/git/wire.rs, CRDT_OVERHAUL.md
design:
**Design**
Choose one canonical ordering and enforce it:
- Preferred: implement Ord for DepKind by as_str() lexical order.
- Alternatively: document enum order as canonical and add tests to lock it.
Update docs accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Unit test: DepKind ordering matches chosen canonical order.\n- [ ] Git wire test: serialized deps.jsonl order is stable and matches canonical order.\n- [ ] CRDT_OVERHAUL.md updated if spec language changes.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
MSRV guard: enforce compiler support for let-chains
description:
**Problem**
Code uses if-let chains; lowering MSRV below their stabilization would break builds without clear failure mode.

**Files**: Cargo.toml, tests/* (new test module)
design:
**Design**
Add a small compile-time guard using rustversion (dev-dependency):
- In a test module, add #[rustversion::before(1.64)] compile_error!("MSRV must be >= 1.64 for let-chains").
This makes any MSRV regression fail immediately. Keep Cargo.toml rust-version in sync.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Test/compile guard exists and fails on compilers < 1.64.\n- [ ] rust-version in Cargo.toml remains >= 1.64 (currently 1.91).
closed_reason: Paused per request before starting work

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
title: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
Encode known/unknown head state in Watermark types
description:
**Problem**
`HeadStatus::Unknown` exists in core and can be constructed for any watermark, yet several runtime paths treat it as impossible and `unreachable!()` if it appears. This means the type admits a state the system claims cannot happen. It violates “types tell the truth” and creates latent panics if a future refactor or new codepath produces Unknown.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-core/src/watermark.rs:118` — `HeadStatus` includes `Unknown`.
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs:727` — `unreachable!("unknown head should never reach session state")`.
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs:287` — same assumption.
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/executor.rs:840` — “durable watermark head should be known”.

Severity: latent panic in WAL/repl and mutation pipelines; correctness depends on a convention rather than an enforced invariant.
design:
**Design**
Encode “known head” vs “unknown head” in types rather than runtime branches.

Option A (split enum):
- `enum HeadKnown { Genesis, Known([u8;32]) }`
- `enum HeadMaybe { Genesis, Known([u8;32]), Unknown }`
- `Watermark<K, H>` where H is `HeadKnown` or `HeadMaybe`.
- Functions that require known heads (durable/applied in repl/gap buffer) only accept `Watermark<_, HeadKnown>`.

Option B (newtypes):
- `struct DurableHead(HeadStatus)` with `TryFrom<HeadStatus>` enforcing `Genesis|Known`.
- `Watermark<K>` uses `HeadKnown` for durable/applied; only snapshot inputs use `HeadMaybe` and must be validated to `HeadKnown` before entering session state.

Migration:
- Update watermark parsing and session snapshot ingestion to return `Result<Watermark<_, HeadKnown>, WatermarkError>` instead of allowing Unknown.
- Remove `unreachable!` branches; they should become impossible to compile.
- CLI/admin rendering can still show Unknown by using a distinct “maybe head” type if needed.

This follows “types should make time/causality explicit” and “the controversial type is probably the right type.”
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No `unreachable!` or panics for Unknown heads in repl/gap buffer/executor paths.
- [ ] Types distinguish known vs unknown head states; durable/applied paths only accept known.
- [ ] Snapshot ingestion/decoding validates head presence explicitly.
- [ ] All existing tests updated; add one compile-time or conversion test to ensure Unknown cannot reach session state.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
title: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
Clarify prev_sha validation vs gap buffering in v0.5 replication
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN.md v0.5 says receivers must verify `prev_sha256` matches the current head before buffering/appending, but also mandates buffering out-of-order events and issuing WANT. For seq > durable+1, `prev_sha256` references seq-1 which is unknown until the gap fills, so the rule is ambiguous.

**Design**
Clarify the intended rule:
- Option A: allow buffering even if prev_sha is not yet verifiable; validate when the prefix arrives.
- Option B: require prev_sha to match the durable head (no buffering of future events).
Update plan text to remove ambiguity and align the replication/model implementations.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Spec text explicitly states when prev_sha must be validated for buffered events.
- [ ] Model/implementation updated to match clarified rule.

**Files:**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md
- beads_stateright_models/examples/canonical_hash_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/repl_core_machine.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Observability: make metrics emission configurable
description:
**Problem**
Metrics are emitted as `tracing::info!` events and are filtered out unless global log level enables INFO. There is no configuration to enable/disable or tune metric emission.

**Design**
- Introduce `metrics` config (e.g., `metrics.enabled`, `metrics.level`, optional `metrics.target`).
- Add env overrides (e.g., `BD_METRICS`, `BD_METRICS_LEVEL`).
- Update `daemon::metrics` to respect config (no‑op when disabled, emit at configured level/target).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Metrics emission can be toggled and leveled via config/env.
- [ ] Default behavior matches current output for daemon with INFO logging.
- [ ] Tests cover config/env overrides and disabled mode.

**Files:** src/config/schema.rs, src/config/merge.rs, src/telemetry.rs (if needed), src/daemon/metrics.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
Show applied/durable watermarks in admin status CLI output
description:
**Problem**
Admin status human output omits applied/durable watermarks, which are core realtime visibility signals when diagnosing lag. The data exists in API but is hidden in CLI.

**Design**
Extend render_admin_status in src/cli/render.rs to print applied/durable watermarks per namespace/origin in a deterministic order. Keep formatting concise (namespace -> origin -> seq, head state optional). Add a focused unit test for the new formatting.

**Files**
- src/cli/render.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Human output includes applied + durable watermarks grouped by namespace
- [ ] Output order is deterministic (namespace, then origin)
- [ ] Unit test covers the new formatting

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Validate StoreMeta versions + centralize format constants
description:
**Problem**
`StoreRuntime::open` accepts existing `StoreMeta` without validating `store_format_version`, `wal_format_version`, `checkpoint_format_version`, `replication_protocol_version`, or `index_schema_version`. This means a daemon can silently run against an unsupported store layout. Also, format/version constants are duplicated across modules (`store_runtime.rs`, `wal/segment.rs`, `wal/index.rs`, `repl/proto.rs`), which is brittle and can drift.

**Files**
- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
- src/core/store_meta.rs
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs
- src/daemon/repl/proto.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a single source of truth for format/protocol versions (e.g., `core::versions` or `StoreMetaVersions::current()` with associated consts).
- In `StoreRuntime::open`, validate existing `StoreMeta` against the current versions and return a dedicated error (e.g., `StoreRuntimeError::UnsupportedVersion { expected, got }`) on mismatch.
- Update WAL/index/proto modules to import the shared constants instead of redefining their own.
- Add tests to cover mismatch handling and ensure constants are used consistently.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `StoreRuntime::open` rejects incompatible `StoreMeta` versions with a structured error.
- [ ] Only one canonical set of format/protocol constants exists.
- [ ] Tests cover version mismatch and regression on constant drift.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Encode WAL index watermark invariants
description:
**Problem**
WAL index `WatermarkRow` stores seqs and head hashes as independent fields (`applied_seq`, `applied_head_sha`, `durable_seq`, `durable_head_sha`). This makes illegal combinations representable (seq>0 with head=None; seq=0 with head=Some). The invariant is only enforced later (or not at all).

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/index.rs:210` (WatermarkRow)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/index.rs:821` (loading rows without invariant checks)

**Impact**
Index can hold inconsistent watermarks, which can break replication and durability checks. Compiler cannot enforce the invariant.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Represent watermarks as typed `Watermark<K>` (or a `WireWatermark { seq, head }`) in the index row.

- Replace the four raw fields with:
```rust
struct WatermarkRow {
  namespace: NamespaceId,
  origin: ReplicaId,
  applied: Watermark<Applied>,
  durable: Watermark<Durable>,
}
```
- DB decode builds `Watermark<Applied>`/`Watermark<Durable>` via `Watermark::new`, so invalid seq/head combos fail immediately.
- DB encode decomposes the typed watermark into columns.

This enforces seq/head correctness at the type boundary.
acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] `WatermarkRow` cannot be constructed with invalid seq/head combinations.
- [ ] DB load rejects invalid rows before they reach runtime logic.
- [ ] All index users operate on typed watermarks (no raw seq/head pairs).
- [ ] Tests cover row round‑trip and invalid row rejection.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
title: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
Observability: local metrics store (SQLite)
description:
**Problem**
We need local‑first performance visibility without relying on a centralized service. Current metrics are only in-memory snapshots + tracing events.

**Design**
- Add a local observability store (SQLite) for metrics time‑series (and optionally log metadata) under `<data_dir>/obs/obs.db`.
- Record metrics events with timestamp + labels; expose an admin query to fetch ranges.
- Keep storage bounded (retention by time or max rows).
- Optional: store log index/metadata (not full messages) for quick filtering.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Metrics time‑series are persisted locally and queryable via admin API/CLI.
- [ ] Storage is bounded via retention config.
- [ ] Works offline; no external service required.

**Files:** src/daemon/metrics.rs, src/daemon/admin.rs, src/api/admin.rs, src/cli/commands/admin.rs, src/paths.rs
closed_reason: Too much complexity for now; prefer structured logs

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Replace GapBuffer string codes with structured errors
description:
**Problem**
GapBuffer rejects use stringly-typed codes (gap_timeout, gap_buffer_overflow, etc) via IngestDecision::Reject { code: String } in src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs. This bypasses ErrorCode/REALTIME_ERRORS, loses structured details, and makes it easy for codes to drift silently.

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs, src/daemon/repl/session.rs, src/core/error.rs
design:
**Design**
- Introduce a GapReject/GAP error enum that carries ErrorCode plus typed details (e.g., OverloadedDetails or GapDetectedDetails).
- Change IngestDecision::Reject to carry this typed error instead of a String.
- Map the typed error to ErrorPayload in Session (or at the boundary) using canonical ErrorCode values.
- Add tests asserting the payload codes/details for each rejection reason.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No string literals are used to represent gap rejection codes.
- [ ] All gap rejections map to canonical ErrorCode values with details.
- [ ] Tests cover the mapping for timeout/overflow cases.

Add LoadedRemote proof type to eliminate repo_state unwraps

darin@book
title: darin@book
Repl FrameReader must enforce negotiated max_frame_bytes
description:
**Problem**
We construct `FrameReader` with `limits.max_frame_bytes` and never update it after handshake. This means we accept inbound frames larger than the negotiated `peer.max_frame_bytes` (as long as they are below the local max). That violates the handshake contract and makes the negotiated frame limit a lie.

This is an implicit invariant: code assumes the negotiated limit is enforced, but the type does not encode it and the reader keeps the pre-handshake limit forever.

**Impact**
- Protocol correctness: peers can send frames larger than agreed without being rejected.
- Resource safety: negotiated limit is ignored, enabling larger-than-expected frames.
- Hard-to-see bug because the limit looks enforced elsewhere (`SessionWire::frame_limit`), but only for *sending*.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/frame.rs` (FrameReader)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/manager.rs` (run_reader_loop outbound)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/server.rs` (run_reader_loop inbound)
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Make the negotiated frame limit a type boundary, not a runtime convention.

1) Introduce typestated reader limits:
```rust
struct Unnegotiated;
struct Negotiated { max_frame_bytes: usize }
struct FrameReader<R, L> { reader: R, limit: L }
```
- `FrameReader<R, Unnegotiated>` is only valid before handshake.
- After handshake, transition to `FrameReader<R, Negotiated>` using the negotiated max.

2) Split reader loop helpers:
- `run_reader_loop_unnegotiated(reader, ...)` for the initial HELLO.
- `run_reader_loop_negotiated(reader, negotiated_max, ...)` for steady-state.

3) Alternatively, if typestate feels heavy, add a `FrameLimit` newtype and make `run_reader_loop` require it; only the handshake produces a `FrameLimit` for use.

4) Enforce `min(local_max, peer_max)` as the negotiated limit; reject larger frames with `FrameTooLarge`.

This removes scatter: the negotiated limit is enforced where frames are read, not assumed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] After handshake, inbound/outbound reader loops enforce the negotiated `max_frame_bytes`.
- [ ] A peer sending frames larger than negotiated is rejected with `FrameTooLarge`.
- [ ] The negotiated limit is produced by the handshake and required by the reader API (compile-time gating).
- [ ] Tests cover: negotiated limit < local max; oversized frame rejected.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.

title: Smart commit message threshold (5) is hardcoded
description:
## What's Wrong
The threshold for switching from detailed to count-based commit messages is hardcoded to 5 in to_commit_message().

## Where
- src/git/sync.rs:92 (`if !self.details.is_empty() && total <= 5`)

## Why It Matters
Minor - the magic number 5 isn't configurable or documented. Fine for now, but if users want different behavior they can't change it.

## Suggested Fix
Either document why 5, or make it a const at module level.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Repl ACKs must be validated against negotiated namespaces
description:
**Problem**
ACK messages are accepted and forwarded to `PeerAckTable` without validating that their namespaces were negotiated in the handshake. `handle_ack` returns the raw `Ack`, and `update_peer_ack` blindly updates state. A peer can send ACKs for namespaces it never accepted (or was never offered), and we will count those toward durability. This violates parse-don’t-validate and makes durability safety depend on peer honesty.

**Impact**
- False durability success: `DurabilityCoordinator` may count a peer for a namespace it never replicated.
- Compiler cannot prevent misuse; a refactor can accidentally pass raw ACKs further without guards.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs` (message handling + handshake state)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/server.rs` / `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/manager.rs` (`update_peer_ack`)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/peer_acks.rs` (ack table)
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Validate ACKs at the session boundary and make invalid ACKs unrepresentable downstream.

1) Introduce a validated ACK type tied to negotiated namespaces:
```rust
struct ValidatedAck {
  durable: WatermarkState<Durable>,
  applied: Option<WatermarkState<Applied>>,
}
struct AllowedNamespaces(BTreeSet<NamespaceId>);
```
`AllowedNamespaces` is created from the negotiated `SessionPeer.accepted_namespaces`.

2) In `Session<*, Streaming>::handle_ack`, validate: every namespace in `Ack` is in `AllowedNamespaces`.
- If any namespace is not allowed, return `InvalidRequest` / `NamespacePolicyViolation` and transition to `Draining` (fail fast).
- Otherwise, build `ValidatedAck` (optionally filter empty maps) and emit `SessionAction::PeerAck(ValidatedAck)`.

3) Change `SessionAction::PeerAck` to carry `ValidatedAck` (not raw `Ack`).
- Update `update_peer_ack` to accept `ValidatedAck`.
- This makes it impossible to update `PeerAckTable` without negotiated-namespace validation.

4) Preserve ordering/determinism by keeping `BTreeMap` ordering; validation should not reorder.

**Why this is parse-don’t-validate**
Wire ACKs are decoded -> validated immediately -> only validated type flows further. No later runtime checks.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ACKs containing any namespace outside negotiated `accepted_namespaces` are rejected with a clear error.
- [ ] `SessionAction::PeerAck` carries only a validated type (no raw `Ack` in the repl loops).
- [ ] `update_peer_ack` cannot be called without a validated ACK.
- [ ] Ordering of watermark maps remains deterministic (BTreeMap order preserved).
- [ ] Tests cover: allowed ACK, disallowed namespace ACK, mixed allowed+disallowed, and applied=None cases.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
title: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
Add git wire sparse _v and WireBeadFull JSON roundtrip tests
Add integrity checksum for store blobs
description:
**Problem**
State blobs are trusted JSON; corruption is only detected if JSON parsing fails. Silent corruption could lead to inconsistent state or data loss.

**Design**
Include a checksum (e.g., SHA256) in `meta.json` for state/tombstones/deps blobs and verify on read. On mismatch, surface a clear error and avoid overwriting state.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Checksums written on commit
- [ ] Checksums verified on load
- [ ] Corruption returns a structured error

**Files:** src/git/wire.rs, src/git/sync.rs, src/api/mod.rs, SPEC.md
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Allow WAL sync mode override for tests (skip fsync)

darin@book
title: darin@book
Telemetry: logger/tracer interfaces + log retention + trace context
description: Add logging/tracing configuration with file retention + pluggable logger/tracer interfaces. Ensure realtime spans include store/replica/namespace/txn context.
darin@book
title: darin@book
git store JSONL: enforce canonical ordering + uniqueness
description:
Problem
- wire::parse_state_full and friends accept any order and silently overwrite duplicate IDs (state) or duplicate dep keys.
- That means corrupted or hand-edited store files can silently drop data depending on line order.
- The canonical ordering invariants (sorted by BeadId / TombstoneKey / DepKey / NoteId) are not enforced at the parse boundary.

Impact
- Data loss can go unnoticed.
- Violates parse-don't-validate and the "ordering is a stability invariant" we rely on for deterministic diffs.
design:
Design
- Add a canonical JSONL parser for the git store that enforces strictly increasing order and uniqueness per file.
  - state.jsonl: BeadId strictly increasing, no duplicates.
  - tombstones.jsonl: BeadId (or TombstoneKey) strictly increasing, no duplicates.
  - notes.jsonl: (bead_id, note_id) strictly increasing, no duplicates.
  - deps.jsonl: DepKey entries strictly increasing, no duplicates.
- Return WireError::InvalidValue with file + line context on violations.
- Keep serialization order exactly matching these rules so roundtrip is deterministic.

Scatter fit
- Enforce ordering in one parser per file type; eliminate implicit reliance on serialize_* ordering.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/sync.rs (error reporting context)
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- Parsing files with duplicates or out-of-order entries fails with a specific WireError.
- Roundtrip tests verify canonical ordering and strict parsing.
- Existing valid stores continue to parse without changes.

title: mutation: stamp must be derived from actor context
description:
Problem
- MutationEngine::plan accepts a Stamp separate from MutationContext.actor_id and never verifies they match.
- A caller can pass mismatched actor/HLc stamp, yielding events whose HLC actor differs from the request actor.
- This violates causality invariants and is not caught by the compiler.

Impact
- Incorrect event authorship and HLC ordering; difficult-to-debug replication issues.
- Violates "types should tell the truth"; the API implies any Stamp is valid.
design:
Design
- Introduce a StampedContext type that can only be constructed from MutationContext + Stamp after verifying stamp.by == ctx.actor_id.
- Change plan signature to accept StampedContext (or accept a stamp builder that derives from ctx).
- Option: have plan compute stamp from ctx + wall clock, and only accept the wall clock inputs, not a raw Stamp.
- Add a debug assertion or validation error if mismatch is detected in legacy call sites.

Scatter fit
- Make the mismatch unrepresentable; no call site can pass a mismatched stamp.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/executor.rs (callers)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/core.rs (callers)
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- plan cannot be called without a validated StampedContext (compile-time change).
- Mismatched actor/stamp is impossible to represent or yields a clear OpError at construction.
- Tests cover mismatch rejection and valid stamping.

title: Make LoadedStore a real proof via borrowed handle/typestate
description:
**Problem**
`LoadedStore` is a marker type that claims a store is loaded, yet the docs state accessors can still return Internal if the invariant is violated. This is exactly the kind of “type that lies” that causes unsafe refactors and hidden runtime failure modes. It violates the principles in `docs/philosophy/type_design.md`.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/core.rs:54` — `LoadedStore` comment says invariant can still be violated.

Severity: This is the primary gateway to daemon state. Any misuse can lead to internal errors despite “proof” types, making correctness non-local and brittle.
design:
**Design**
Make “loaded store” a real proof by tying it to actual entries in the daemon state via borrowing or a typestate.

Option A (borrowed handle):
- `struct LoadedStore<'a> { store_id: StoreId, remote: RemoteUrl, runtime: &'a mut StoreRuntime, lane: &'a mut GitLaneState }`.
- Only constructed by `Daemon::ensure_repo_loaded*` by looking up entries and borrowing them.
- Callers that need a loaded store take `LoadedStore<'_>`; they can’t exist without the backing map entry.

Option B (index token + invariants):
- `LoadedStore` holds an internal key tied to a `StoreCaches` entry that cannot be fabricated.
- Accessors use the key to retrieve references without `Option` or Internal errors.

Migration:
- Update `ensure_repo_loaded`/`ensure_repo_loaded_strict`/`ensure_repo_fresh` to return the borrowed handle.
- Collapse “internal if invariant violated” branches since they become impossible.
- Add tests for failure modes (ensure errors only occur during loading, not post-hoc access).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A loaded-store handle cannot exist without a backing runtime/lane entry (enforced by borrow or token).
- [ ] Codepaths that currently return Internal due to “missing loaded store” are eliminated.
- [ ] All loaded-store APIs take the new handle type; no raw `store_id`+`remote` pairs used for access.
- [ ] Tests cover missing/invalid store errors at load time only.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
title: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
Speed up git fixtures with a cached template repo
description: Many tests create a fresh git repo via Reinitialized existing Git repository in /Users/darin/Projects/beads-rs-macstudio/.git/ + config. Create a template repo once (per test process) and copy/clone it into temp dirs for each test to avoid repeated init/config overhead. Could use OnceLock + fs copy or git clone --shared.
darin@book
title: darin@book
mutation: dep adds must carry DAG proof types
description:
Problem
- plan_add_dep builds raw DepKey + ad-hoc check_no_cycle, then emits WireDepAddV1.
- The NoCycleProof/DepAddKey types exist but aren't used here, so the compiler can't enforce that DAG checks happened.
- A future refactor can bypass the cycle check without the type system noticing.

Impact
- Cycle invariants for Blocks/Parent depend on discipline instead of types.
- Violates parse-don't-validate and "types should tell the truth".
design:
Design
- Make MutationEngine add deps through DepAddKey only:
  - Use state.check_dep_add_key(key) to return DepAddKey (Acyclic|Free).
  - Change WireDepAddV1 (or an internal wrapper) to carry DepAddKey, or make the add path require a DepAddKey parameter.
- Option A: introduce a ValidatedDepAdd type in core (mirrors ValidatedBeadPatch) that only constructs from DepAddKey; mutation_engine must build that.
- Option B: split add ops in mutation engine: add_dep_acyclic requires NoCycleProof, add_dep_free does not; prohibit raw DepKey in the API.
- Ensure all dependency adds (including create's dependency list and SetParent path) go through the same typed gate.

Scatter fit
- One entry point (check_dep_add_key) owns DAG validity; call sites no longer repeat or forget checks.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- crates/beads-core/src/dep.rs
- crates/beads-core/src/state.rs
- crates/beads-core/src/event.rs (if adding ValidatedDepAdd)
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- There is no mutation path that can add a DAG edge without a DepAddKey/NoCycleProof.
- Compiler forces add paths to handle Acyclic vs Free keys explicitly.
- Tests cover: cycle detection for Blocks/Parent; Related/DiscoveredFrom bypass DAG checks but still validated.
- Existing behavior preserved for valid inputs.

title: LoadGenerator spawns threads even for 1 worker; add fast path
description: tests/integration/fixtures/load_gen.rs always spawns worker threads, even when workers=1 (common in tests). Add a single-thread fast path to avoid thread spawn + atomics/mutex overhead for small loads.
darin@book
title: darin@book
Repl namespace lists must be canonicalized at parse boundary
description:
**Problem**
Replication wire decoding returns raw `Vec<NamespaceId>` for `requested_namespaces`, `offered_namespaces`, and `accepted_namespaces`. Order is preserved from the wire, not canonicalized. Later we compare vectors for equality (e.g. WELCOME replay), which can reject a peer that sends the same set in a different order.

This is a parse-don’t-validate violation (we should normalize at the boundary), and a classic scatter bug: correctness depends on every caller remembering to sort/dedup.

**Impact**
- False handshake failures due to ordering differences.
- Hidden coupling to wire ordering; brittle and hard to reason about.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/proto.rs` (decode_namespace_list)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs` (HELLO/WELCOME replay comparison)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Decoding a namespace list always yields canonical (sorted + deduped) order.
- [ ] Handshake replay accepts the same set regardless of wire ordering.
- [ ] Internal storage uses the canonical type (no raw Vec in session peer).
- [ ] Encoding always emits canonical order.
- [ ] Tests cover: same set different order, duplicates on wire, empty list.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.

title: Repl watermark heads must be coupled to seq at parse boundary
description:
**Problem**
Replication wire structs (`Ack`, `Hello`, `Welcome`) carry `WatermarkMap` plus optional `WatermarkHeads`. There is no type-level coupling between `seq` and `head`, so it is easy to construct invalid states (e.g., `seq>0` with no head). The compiler cannot prevent this; errors only surface later as runtime failures (`MissingHead` / divergence), and these failures are not localized to the parsing boundary.

This violates parse-don’t-validate and makes correctness depend on call-site discipline.

**Impact**
- Incorrect or missing heads can stall replication or mark peers diverged.
- Internal code can accidentally emit invalid ACKs without compile-time feedback.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/proto.rs` (Ack/Hello/Welcome decode)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/session.rs` (watermark snapshot/seed)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/repl/peer_acks.rs`
design:
**Design**
Introduce a typed, invariant-preserving watermark representation at the Repl boundary:

- Create an internal type that represents *validated* watermarks (use `Watermarks<K>` or a new `WatermarkSnapshot<K>` that contains `Watermark<K>` values).
- Keep wire format unchanged by introducing wire-only structs:
  - `WireAck` / `WireHello` / `WireWelcome` with `WatermarkMap` + `WatermarkHeads` (serde/cbor only)
  - `Ack` / `Hello` / `Welcome` used internally with `Watermarks<K>` (or validated snapshot type)
- `decode_*` should parse into wire structs, then immediately validate/convert into the typed representation:
  - Missing head for seq>0 becomes a `ProtoDecodeError::InvalidField`.
  - Genesis (`seq=0`) must have no head (or Genesis marker), enforced by `Watermark::new`.
- `encode_*` should accept the typed representation and derive wire maps/heads in canonical order.

**Ordering / invariants preserved**
- Maintain deterministic ordering by using `BTreeMap` during wire conversion.
- No change to wire encoding order or shape; only validation is moved to the parse boundary.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Internal repl message types cannot be constructed without valid head/seq combinations.
- [ ] Decode rejects `seq>0` without head and `seq=0` with head (clear error).
- [ ] Encoding preserves canonical ordering and wire compatibility.
- [ ] Tests cover valid/invalid ACKs and HELLO/WELCOME watermark snapshots.
- [ ] `cargo test` passes.

description: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
title: darin@darins-Mac-Studio-2.local
Extract ValidationError helper for CLI handlers
IPC update patch must not allow Closed status
description:
**Problem**
The IPC update patch schema allows setting `status=Closed` via `BeadPatch.status`, without requiring `closed_reason`/`closed_on_branch`. This violates the workflow invariant (closed must carry closure data), and relies on downstream runtime validation to reject invalid updates.

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ops.rs:82` — `BeadPatch.status: Patch<WorkflowStatus>`.
- `crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/payload.rs:51` — Update payload uses `BeadPatch`.

**Impact**
IPC schema can represent illegal states; compiler can’t prevent accidental Close‑via‑Update.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Make “Close” a dedicated operation only; updates must not encode the Closed state.

Options:
- Replace `BeadPatch.status: Patch<WorkflowStatus>` with `Patch<OpenInProgress>` where:
```rust
enum OpenInProgress { Open, InProgress }
```
- Alternatively, remove `status` from `BeadPatch` entirely and require `Close`/`Reopen` IPC ops for status transitions.

This forces correct closure semantics and makes illegal updates unrepresentable at the schema level.
acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] IPC update patch cannot encode `Closed` (compile‑time enforced).
- [ ] Closing a bead is only possible via the `Close` IPC operation with explicit reason/branch data.
- [ ] Attempts to send `status=closed` in Update payload fail at decode time.
- [ ] Tests cover Update payload rejecting closed status and Close op still working.

title: Git fetch should use explicit refspec + surface errors
description:
**Problem**
`remote.fetch(&["refs/heads/beads/store"], ...)` relies on libgit2 refspec defaults; it may only update FETCH_HEAD and leave `refs/remotes/origin/beads/store` stale. Fetch errors are also ignored, so the daemon can sync against stale remote state without any signal.

**Design**
Use an explicit refspec (`refs/heads/beads/store:refs/remotes/origin/beads/store`) and return/propagate fetch errors (or at least surface them in status/IPC). Keep a best-effort path for offline reads but make failures visible.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Fetch updates `refs/remotes/origin/beads/store`
- [ ] Fetch errors are surfaced (status/IPC/logs)
- [ ] Tests cover refspec + fetch failure behavior

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/daemon/git_worker.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
init_beads_ref must not overwrite existing local ref
description:
**Problem**
`init_beads_ref` fetches and force-updates `refs/heads/beads/store` to remote even if a local ref already exists. If local has data, this can overwrite it.

**Design**
If local ref exists, treat init as a no-op (already initialized). Only fast-forward local when it does not exist. Never force-update without preserving local history. Add a test that ensures init does not modify an existing local ref.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] init is a no-op when local ref exists
- [ ] No forced updates on existing local refs
- [ ] Tests cover init with existing local ref
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Scheduler spawns unbounded threads on reschedule (threadpocalypse)
description:
**Problem**
`src/daemon/scheduler.rs` spawns a new thread on every schedule call, and reschedules do not cancel old sleepers. Under bursty ops, this devolves into "death by a thousand sleeping threads".

**Design**
Single timer mechanism:
- Keep a min-heap of `(deadline, remote)`
- State loop computes next deadline and uses `crossbeam::channel::after(next - now)` (or one dedicated timer thread)
- When rescheduling, update the heap entry (or push and ignore stale entries by checking current deadline in a map)

This makes the daemon "boring" which is good.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] At most 1 timer thread (or zero with channel-based approach)
- [ ] Rapid reschedules dont accumulate sleeping threads
- [ ] Stress test: 1000 rapid mutations, verify thread count stays bounded
- [ ] Existing sync behavior preserved (debounce still works)

**Files:** src/daemon/scheduler.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
Default namespace policies are incomplete (missing sys/wf/tmp)
description:
**Problem**
When `namespaces.toml` is missing, we default to only `core` instead of the spec default set (`core`, `sys`, `wf`, `tmp`). This makes new installs diverge from the realtime plan and forces callers to handle “missing namespace” errors that shouldn’t exist by default.

**Signals / Evidence**
- `default_policies()` returns only `core` (`src/daemon/store_runtime.rs`).
- Spec defaults for `sys`, `wf`, `tmp` are documented in REALTIME_PLAN.md (0.4).

**Why this hurts velocity**
We keep tripping over missing namespaces when adding features that expect them (policy checks, replication routing, GC/retention). It’s a hidden configuration requirement that should be encoded in defaults.
design:
**Design**
- Expand `default_policies()` to include `sys_default`, `wf_default`, `tmp_default` with spec values.
- Add a test that missing `namespaces.toml` yields all four namespaces.
- Ensure any code that assumes only `core` is updated or guarded.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Default policies include `core`, `sys`, `wf`, and `tmp`.
- [ ] Test covers default policy set when `namespaces.toml` is absent.

darin@book
title: darin@book
Bind WAL VerifiedRecord to canonical event bytes/body
description:
**Problem**
`VerifiedRecord` is “verified” only relative to a provided `EventBody`, but it doesn’t carry that body or ensure its payload bytes are the canonical encoding of that body. Callers can accidentally pair a header/payload with the wrong decoded body (or a non-canonical body encoding) while still constructing `VerifiedRecord`. This undermines the WAL’s trust boundary and violates the “information holds its shape” principle.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/record.rs:330` — verification uses `EventBody` passed from outside.
- `crates/beads-core/src/event.rs:32` — `EventBytes<Canonical>` type exists but is not used in WAL record types.

Severity: a class of subtle integrity bugs where WAL frames are “verified” without actually binding bytes to semantic content.
design:
**Design**
Make `VerifiedRecord` carry a canonical event representation that binds body ↔ bytes ↔ hash.

Option A (canonical event wrapper):
- Introduce `CanonicalEvent { body: EventBody<Validated>, bytes: EventBytes<Canonical>, sha256: Sha256 }`.
- `VerifiedRecord` stores `CanonicalEvent` (or at least `EventBytes<Canonical>` + `sha256`), eliminating mismatched pairs.

Option B (generic record state):
- `Record<State>` where `State = Verified<Canonical>` includes `EventBytes<Canonical>` and `EventBody<Validated>`.
- `verify_with_event_body` replaced by `verify_with_canonical(bytes: EventBytes<Canonical>, body: EventBody<Validated>)`.

Migration:
- Update WAL write APIs to require `CanonicalEvent` or `EventBytes<Canonical>`.
- Update replay/ingest to decode bytes into canonical event (already enforced in core decode) and thread that through.

Notes:
- This should dovetail with the existing P0 bead to add `EventBody<Validated>`.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `VerifiedRecord` cannot be constructed without canonical bytes bound to the exact event body.
- [ ] WAL append APIs require canonical event bytes (or a canonical event wrapper).
- [ ] Existing replay/ingest code updated; no ad‑hoc pairing of body + payload remains.
- [ ] Tests updated or added to ensure mismatched body/payload cannot compile or fails at conversion boundary.

title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan

**Problem**
`Daemon::apply_update` applies `BeadPatch` field-by-field inline. The patch semantics live in `src/daemon/ops.rs`, but the actual application logic lives in `src/daemon/executor.rs`, so adding or changing fields requires touching both places and is easy to miss.

**Design**
Add a helper on `BeadPatch` (e.g., `apply_to_fields(&self, fields: &mut BeadFields, stamp: &Stamp) -> Result<(), OpError>`) in `src/daemon/ops.rs`. Move the current field-application logic (including label parsing and status→workflow transition) into this helper. Update `Daemon::apply_update` to call the helper inside `apply_wal_mutation`.

**Design Notes**
Keep behavior identical: ignore `Patch::Clear` for required fields (title/description) and for labels (no-op), preserve current status validation and error messages.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `Daemon::apply_update` delegates patch application to `BeadPatch::apply_to_fields`.
- [ ] Label validation and workflow transitions behave exactly as before.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/ops.rs`, `src/daemon/executor.rs`

darin@book
title: darin@book
rustfmt.toml uses unstable options on stable
description:
## What's Wrong
cargo fmt prints warnings because rustfmt.toml sets unstable_features=true and group_imports=StdExternalCrate, which require nightly rustfmt.

## Where
- rustfmt.toml

## Why It Matters
Noisy output in CI/local workflows (for example, just release-patch runs cargo fmt -- --check). It can also cause formatting drift if contributors have different toolchains.

## Options
1. Remove unstable settings and rely on stable rustfmt defaults.
2. Pin nightly rustfmt in dev shell/CI to support the config.

---
Discovered while: running cargo fmt during migration slug work.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
git store meta parse should be versioned + verified
description:
Problem
- wire::parse_meta exposes format_version but no caller enforces it; read_state_at_oid always treats store as legacy/v1.
- meta.json can omit checksums; we silently skip verification and still accept blobs.
- root_slug is Option<String> and not validated at the parse boundary; invalid slugs propagate until mutation_engine rejects them at runtime.

Impact
- We can accept incompatible future formats or corrupt data without a clear error.
- The type system can't tell us whether meta was verified or even compatible.
- Violates parse-don't-validate and Scatter (callers must remember what is safe).
design:
Design
- Replace ParsedMeta with a versioned enum, e.g. StoreMeta::Legacy | StoreMeta::V1 { root_slug: Option<BeadSlug>, last_write_stamp: Option<WriteStamp>, checksums: StoreChecksums }.
- parse_meta validates format_version == 1; unknown versions return WireError::InvalidValue (or a new UnsupportedVersion variant).
- For v1 meta, require all checksum fields; treat partial checksum sets as hard errors.
- Validate root_slug via BeadSlug::parse at parse time; store as Option<BeadSlug> so invalid slugs are unrepresentable.
- read_state_at_oid returns a typed LoadedStore that carries verification state explicitly (e.g. LoadedStore { meta: StoreMeta, state }). Callers must handle Legacy vs V1 (no silent fallthrough).

Scatter fit
- Put all version + checksum validation in wire.rs; callers should not need to re-check.
- Remove ad-hoc Option<String> plumbing for root_slug; let the type tell the truth.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/sync.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/git_worker.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/migration.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/core.rs
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- parse_meta rejects unsupported format_version with a clear error.
- v1 meta without full checksum set fails to parse.
- root_slug is validated on load and stored as BeadSlug (invalid slug cannot reach mutation_engine).
- read_state_at_oid exposes meta verification state via types (Legacy vs V1) and callers compile-fail until they handle it.
- Add tests for unsupported version, missing checksums, invalid root_slug, and a happy-path v1 meta with checksums.

title: Store lock heartbeat is never updated
description:
**Problem**
Store lock metadata includes `last_heartbeat_ms`, but the daemon never updates it. That makes the lock look stale even when the daemon is healthy, and it undermines the intended recovery UX.

**Signals / Evidence**
- `StoreLockMeta` contains `last_heartbeat_ms` (`src/daemon/store_lock.rs`).
- `StoreLock::update_heartbeat` exists but is never called (no references).

**Why this hurts velocity**
Operators will get misleading diagnostics and may delete live locks. It’s also a sign of unexercised code paths.
design:
**Design**
- Add a small periodic heartbeat in the daemon loop (e.g., every 10s) that calls `StoreLock::update_heartbeat`.
- If we don’t want heartbeats, remove the field + method entirely to avoid lying metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `last_heartbeat_ms` is regularly updated while the daemon holds the lock, or the field is removed.
- [ ] Tests or logs demonstrate the heartbeat behavior.

description: darin@book

Add BD_TEST_FAST to shorten debounces/backoffs/keepalives for tests

Apply in config merge or daemon defaults so integration tests don’t pay production timers.

Remote URL normalization should canonicalize ssh/https variants

State sharing keys by normalized remote URL, but normalization is currently minimal (trim slash + parse). ssh/https variants (e.g., `git@github.com:org/repo` vs `https://github.com/org/repo`) can create split state.

**Design**
